{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[ja:念仏寺山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun (念仏寺山古墳) |image = [[File:Kaika Tennnouryou.png|260px]]
Orthographic aerial view of Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun
{{small|{{Geospatial Orthographic Map}}}} |Alternate name = Kōbō-yama Kofun / Sakaue-yama Kofun |Cluster = {{ill|Itsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|率川古墳群}}Dramatic Nara, p.151Refer to materials from the Nara City Board of Education, Archaeological Section. |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, Aburakōji-chō |Latitude = 34°41′0.49″N |Longitude = 135°49′26.44″E |ISO = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Scale = Total mound length approx. 100m
(Estimated restoration: 115-120m)
Height approx. 8m (rear circular mound) |Burial Chamber = Unknown |Construction Period = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, 9th emperor |Artifacts = Fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} |Imperial Mausoleum = {{ill|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum|ja|春日率川坂上陵}} |Special Notes = |Map = Nara city |Label = Nenbutsuji-yama
Kofun |Label Position = bottom |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-SSW }} [[File:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun, haisho-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}'s Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum]] '''Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun''' (念仏寺山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Aburakōji-chō|ja|油阪町}}, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "'''Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum'''" (春日率川坂上陵) and attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, the 9th emperor of Japan. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tumulus is located in central Nara City, between {{ill|Sanjo Street|ja|三条通り}} and Aburakōji Street.{{Cite book|title=Nara City Historical Records, Archaeology Volume|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1971|pages=82-85}} It has not undergone extensive excavation due to its designation as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. The tumulus is oriented south-southeast. During the {{ill|Early Modern Period|ja|近世}}, the tumulus fell under the temple grounds of {{ill|Nenbutsuji Temple (Nara City)|ja|念仏寺 (奈良市)}} and was utilized as a cemetery, leading to mound alterations. In the late Edo period, cemetery relocation and restoration of the mausoleum took place.{{Cite book|title=Nara Prefecture Archaeological History|publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|year=2001}} Current features include flattened tops of the circular and trapezoidal sections, and the mound's original stepped construction is no longer evident. === Artifacts and Age === Fragments of cylindrical haniwa discovered on the western embankment suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century (middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}).{{Cite book|title=Historical Atlas of Japan|publisher=平凡社|year=1981}} Excavations in 1975 and 2008 uncovered Edo-period burial artifacts, including ossuary vessels and wooden coffins.[http://www.47news.jp/CN/200812/CN2008121101000797.html "Artifacts from Edo Period Uncovered near Emperor Kaika's Mausoleum"] (Kyodo News, December 11, 2008). During the construction of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, many ancient Kofuns were destroyed. However, Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun and a few others, such as the {{ill|Hōraisan Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳}}, survived.[https://web.archive.org/web/20130615225332/http://www.47news.jp/CN/201303/CN2013032501001891.html "Ancient Tombs Destroyed for Heijō-kyō Construction"] (Kyodo News, March 25, 2013). == Archaeological Record == * Meiji Period: Designated as Emperor Kaika’s mausoleum by the Imperial Household Agency. * 1975: Excavation during torii reconstruction revealed Edo-period burial items. * 1976: Pre-construction survey of embankments. * 1988: Structural maintenance of protective fencing and gates. * 2002: Investigations during road construction. * 2008: Reconstruction of torii uncovered ossuary vessels, wooden coffins, and human remains. == Mound Dimensions == * Total length: Approx. 100 meters * Rear circular mound: ** Diameter: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 8 meters * Front trapezoidal mound: ** Width: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 6 meters == Buried Individual == The actual individual interred at Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Kaika, the 9th emperor of Japan.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Mausolea - Imperial Household Agency] Ancient records, such as the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}* and *{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}*, reference the location as "Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue," supporting this identification. However, Emperor Kaika and other rulers of the {{ill|Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|ja|欠史八代}} are considered mythological or semi-legendary figures.{{Cite book|title=Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum Overview|publisher=平凡社|year=2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dramatic Nara|author=Ogura Tsukiko|publisher=京阪奈情報教育出版|year=2010}} * {{Cite book|title=Guide to Imperial Mausolea and Ancient Tombs|author=Mori Hiroshi|publisher=筑摩書房|year=2011}} * {{Cite book|title=Continuation of Japan’s Ancient Tombs|author=Nishizaki Takuya|publisher=東京堂出版|year=2002}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}} * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|207421770|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/009/index.html Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[ja:念仏寺山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun (念仏寺山古墳) |image = [[File:Kaika Tennnouryou.png|260px]]
Orthographic aerial view of Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun
{{small|{{Geospatial Orthographic Map}}}} |Alternate name = Kōbō-yama Kofun / Sakaue-yama Kofun |Cluster = {{ill|Itsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|率川古墳群}}Dramatic Nara, p.151Refer to materials from the Nara City Board of Education, Archaeological Section. |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, Aburakōji-chō |Latitude = 34°41′0.49″N |Longitude = 135°49′26.44″E |ISO = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Scale = Total mound length approx. 100m
(Estimated restoration: 115-120m)
Height approx. 8m (rear circular mound) |Burial Chamber = Unknown |Construction Period = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, 9th emperor |burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} |Imperial Mausoleum = {{ill|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum|ja|春日率川坂上陵}} |Special Notes = |Map = Nara city |Label = Nenbutsuji-yama
Kofun |Label Position = bottom |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-SSW }} [[File:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun, haisho-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}'s Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum]] '''Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun''' (念仏寺山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Aburakōji-chō|ja|油阪町}}, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "'''Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum'''" (春日率川坂上陵) and attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, the 9th emperor of Japan. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tumulus is located in central Nara City, between {{ill|Sanjo Street|ja|三条通り}} and Aburakōji Street.{{Cite book|title=Nara City Historical Records, Archaeology Volume|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1971|pages=82-85}} It has not undergone extensive excavation due to its designation as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. The tumulus is oriented south-southeast. During the {{ill|Early Modern Period|ja|近世}}, the tumulus fell under the temple grounds of {{ill|Nenbutsuji Temple (Nara City)|ja|念仏寺 (奈良市)}} and was utilized as a cemetery, leading to mound alterations. In the late Edo period, cemetery relocation and restoration of the mausoleum took place.{{Cite book|title=Nara Prefecture Archaeological History|publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|year=2001}} Current features include flattened tops of the circular and trapezoidal sections, and the mound's original stepped construction is no longer evident. === Artifacts and Age === Fragments of cylindrical haniwa discovered on the western embankment suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century (middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}).{{Cite book|title=Historical Atlas of Japan|publisher=平凡社|year=1981}} Excavations in 1975 and 2008 uncovered Edo-period burial artifacts, including ossuary vessels and wooden coffins.[http://www.47news.jp/CN/200812/CN2008121101000797.html "Artifacts from Edo Period Uncovered near Emperor Kaika's Mausoleum"] (Kyodo News, December 11, 2008). During the construction of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, many ancient Kofuns were destroyed. However, Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun and a few others, such as the {{ill|Hōraisan Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳}}, survived.[https://web.archive.org/web/20130615225332/http://www.47news.jp/CN/201303/CN2013032501001891.html "Ancient Tombs Destroyed for Heijō-kyō Construction"] (Kyodo News, March 25, 2013). == Archaeological Record == * Meiji Period: Designated as Emperor Kaika’s mausoleum by the Imperial Household Agency. * 1975: Excavation during torii reconstruction revealed Edo-period burial items. * 1976: Pre-construction survey of embankments. * 1988: Structural maintenance of protective fencing and gates. * 2002: Investigations during road construction. * 2008: Reconstruction of torii uncovered ossuary vessels, wooden coffins, and human remains. == Mound Dimensions == * Total length: Approx. 100 meters * Rear circular mound: ** Diameter: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 8 meters * Front trapezoidal mound: ** Width: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 6 meters == Buried Individual == The actual individual interred at Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Kaika, the 9th emperor of Japan.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Mausolea - Imperial Household Agency] Ancient records, such as the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}* and *{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}*, reference the location as "Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue," supporting this identification. However, Emperor Kaika and other rulers of the {{ill|Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|ja|欠史八代}} are considered mythological or semi-legendary figures.{{Cite book|title=Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum Overview|publisher=平凡社|year=2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dramatic Nara|author=Ogura Tsukiko|publisher=京阪奈情報教育出版|year=2010}} * {{Cite book|title=Guide to Imperial Mausolea and Ancient Tombs|author=Mori Hiroshi|publisher=筑摩書房|year=2011}} * {{Cite book|title=Continuation of Japan’s Ancient Tombs|author=Nishizaki Takuya|publisher=東京堂出版|year=2002}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}} * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|207421770|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/009/index.html Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[ja:念仏寺山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun (念仏寺山古墳) |image = [[File:Kaika Tennnouryou.png|260px]]
Orthographic aerial view of Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun
{{small|{{Geospatial Orthographic Map}}}} |Alternate name = Kōbō-yama Kofun / Sakaue-yama Kofun |Cluster = {{ill|Itsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|率川古墳群}}Dramatic Nara, p.151Refer to materials from the Nara City Board of Education, Archaeological Section. |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, Aburakōji-chō |Latitude = 34°41′0.49″N |Longitude = 135°49′26.44″E |ISO = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Scale = Total mound length approx. 100m
(Estimated restoration: 115-120m)
Height approx. 8m (rear circular mound) |Burial Chamber = Unknown |Construction Period = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, 9th emperor |burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} |Imperial Mausoleum = {{ill|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum|ja|春日率川坂上陵}} |Special Notes = |Map = Nara city |Label = Nenbutsuji-yama
Kofun |Label Position = bottom |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-SSW }} [[File:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun, haisho-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}'s Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum]] '''Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun''' (念仏寺山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Aburakōji-chō|ja|油阪町}}, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "'''Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum'''" (春日率川坂上陵) and attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, the 9th emperor of Japan. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tumulus is located in central Nara City, between {{ill|Sanjo Street|ja|三条通り}} and Aburakōji Street.{{Cite book|title=Nara City Historical Records, Archaeology Volume|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1971|pages=82-85}} It has not undergone extensive excavation due to its designation as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. The tumulus is oriented south-southeast. During the {{ill|Early Modern Period|ja|近世}}, the tumulus fell under the temple grounds of {{ill|Nenbutsuji Temple (Nara City)|ja|念仏寺 (奈良市)}} and was utilized as a cemetery, leading to mound alterations. In the late Edo period, cemetery relocation and restoration of the mausoleum took place.{{Cite book|title=Nara Prefecture Archaeological History|publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|year=2001}} Current features include flattened tops of the circular and trapezoidal sections, and the mound's original stepped construction is no longer evident. === Artifacts and Age === Fragments of cylindrical haniwa discovered on the western embankment suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century (middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}).{{Cite book|title=Historical Atlas of Japan|publisher=平凡社|year=1981}} Excavations in 1975 and 2008 uncovered Edo-period burial artifacts, including ossuary vessels and wooden coffins.[http://www.47news.jp/CN/200812/CN2008121101000797.html "Artifacts from Edo Period Uncovered near Emperor Kaika's Mausoleum"] (Kyodo News, December 11, 2008). During the construction of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, many ancient Kofuns were destroyed. However, Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun and a few others, such as the {{ill|Hōraisan Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳}}, survived.[https://web.archive.org/web/20130615225332/http://www.47news.jp/CN/201303/CN2013032501001891.html "Ancient Tombs Destroyed for Heijō-kyō Construction"] (Kyodo News, March 25, 2013). == Archaeological Record == * Meiji Period: Designated as Emperor Kaika’s mausoleum by the Imperial Household Agency. * 1975: Excavation during torii reconstruction revealed Edo-period burial items. * 1976: Pre-construction survey of embankments. * 1988: Structural maintenance of protective fencing and gates. * 2002: Investigations during road construction. * 2008: Reconstruction of torii uncovered ossuary vessels, wooden coffins, and human remains. == Mound Dimensions == * Total length: Approx. 100 meters * Rear circular mound: ** Diameter: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 8 meters * Front trapezoidal mound: ** Width: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 6 meters == Buried Individual == The actual individual interred at Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Kaika, the 9th emperor of Japan.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Mausolea - Imperial Household Agency] Ancient records, such as the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}* and *{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}*, reference the location as "Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue," supporting this identification. However, Emperor Kaika and other rulers of the {{ill|Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|ja|欠史八代}} are considered mythological or semi-legendary figures.{{Cite book|title=Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum Overview|publisher=平凡社|year=2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dramatic Nara|author=Ogura Tsukiko|publisher=京阪奈情報教育出版|year=2010}} * {{Cite book|title=Guide to Imperial Mausolea and Ancient Tombs|author=Mori Hiroshi|publisher=筑摩書房|year=2011}} * {{Cite book|title=Continuation of Japan’s Ancient Tombs|author=Nishizaki Takuya|publisher=東京堂出版|year=2002}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}} * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|207421770|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/009/index.html Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] In the late Edo period, cemetery relocation and restoration of the mausoleum took place.{{Cite book|title=Nara Prefecture Archaeological History|publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|year=2001}} Current features include flattened tops of the circular and trapezoidal sections, and the mound's original stepped construction is no longer evident. === Artifacts and Age === Fragments of cylindrical haniwa discovered on the western embankment suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century (middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}).{{Cite book|title=Historical Atlas of Japan|publisher=平凡社|year=1981}} Excavations in 1975 and 2008 uncovered Edo-period burial artifacts, including ossuary vessels and wooden coffins.[http://www.47news.jp/CN/200812/CN2008121101000797.html "Artifacts from Edo Period Uncovered near Emperor Kaika's Mausoleum"] (Kyodo News, December 11, 2008). During the construction of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, many ancient Kofuns were destroyed. However, Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun and a few others, such as the {{ill|Hōraisan Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳}}, survived.[https://web.archive.org/web/20130615225332/http://www.47news.jp/CN/201303/CN2013032501001891.html "Ancient Tombs Destroyed for Heijō-kyō Construction"] (Kyodo News, March 25, 2013). == Archaeological Record == * Meiji Period: Designated as Emperor Kaika’s mausoleum by the Imperial Household Agency. * 1975: Excavation during torii reconstruction revealed Edo-period burial items. * 1976: Pre-construction survey of embankments. * 1988: Structural maintenance of protective fencing and gates. * 2002: Investigations during road construction. * 2008: Reconstruction of torii uncovered ossuary vessels, wooden coffins, and human remains. == Mound Dimensions == * Total length: Approx. 100 meters * Rear circular mound: ** Diameter: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 8 meters * Front trapezoidal mound: ** Width: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 6 meters == Buried Individual == The actual individual interred at Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Kaika, the 9th emperor of Japan.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Mausolea - Imperial Household Agency] Ancient records, such as the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}* and *{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}*, reference the location as "Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue," supporting this identification. However, Emperor Kaika and other rulers of the {{ill|Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|ja|欠史八代}} are considered mythological or semi-legendary figures.{{Cite book|title=Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum Overview|publisher=平凡社|year=2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dramatic Nara|author=Ogura Tsukiko|publisher=京阪奈情報教育出版|year=2010}} * {{Cite book|title=Guide to Imperial Mausolea and Ancient Tombs|author=Mori Hiroshi|publisher=筑摩書房|year=2011}} * {{Cite book|title=Continuation of Japan’s Ancient Tombs|author=Nishizaki Takuya|publisher=東京堂出版|year=2002}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}} * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|207421770|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/009/index.html Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[ja:念仏寺山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun (念仏寺山古墳) |image = [[File:Kaika Tennnouryou.png|260px]]
Orthographic aerial view of Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun
{{small|{{Geospatial Orthographic map}}}} |Alternate name = Kōbō-yama Kofun / Sakaue-yama Kofun |Cluster = {{ill|Itsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|率川古墳群}}Dramatic Nara, p.151Refer to materials from the Nara City Board of Education, Archaeological Section. |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, Aburakōji-chō |Latitude = 34°41′0.49″N |Longitude = 135°49′26.44″E |ISO = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |map_size = Total mound length approx. 100m
(Estimated restoration: 115-120m)
Height approx. 8m (rear circular mound) |Burial Chamber = Unknown |built = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Burial = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, 9th emperor |burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} |Imperial Mausoleum = {{ill|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum|ja|春日率川坂上陵}} |Special Notes = |map = Nara city |map_dot_label = Nenbutsuji-yama
Kofun |map_dot_label Position = bottom |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-SSW }} [[File:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun, haisho-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}'s Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum]] '''Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun''' (念仏寺山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Aburakōji-chō|ja|油阪町}}, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "'''Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum'''" (春日率川坂上陵) and attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, the 9th emperor of Japan. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tumulus is located in central Nara City, between {{ill|Sanjo Street|ja|三条通り}} and Aburakōji Street.{{Cite book|title=Nara City Historical Records, Archaeology Volume|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1971|pages=82-85}} It has not undergone extensive excavation due to its designation as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. The tumulus is oriented south-southeast. During the {{ill|Early Modern Period|ja|近世}}, the tumulus fell under the temple grounds of {{ill|Nenbutsuji Temple (Nara City)|ja|念仏寺 (奈良市)}} and was utilized as a cemetery, leading to mound alterations. In the late Edo period, cemetery relocation and restoration of the mausoleum took place.{{Cite book|title=Nara Prefecture Archaeological History|publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|year=2001}} Current features include flattened tops of the circular and trapezoidal sections, and the mound's original stepped construction is no longer evident. === Artifacts and Age === Fragments of cylindrical haniwa discovered on the western embankment suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century (middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}).{{Cite book|title=Historical Atlas of Japan|publisher=平凡社|year=1981}} Excavations in 1975 and 2008 uncovered Edo-period burial artifacts, including ossuary vessels and wooden coffins.[http://www.47news.jp/CN/200812/CN2008121101000797.html "Artifacts from Edo Period Uncovered near Emperor Kaika's Mausoleum"] (Kyodo News, December 11, 2008). During the construction of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, many ancient Kofuns were destroyed. However, Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun and a few others, such as the {{ill|Hōraisan Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳}}, survived.[https://web.archive.org/web/20130615225332/http://www.47news.jp/CN/201303/CN2013032501001891.html "Ancient Tombs Destroyed for Heijō-kyō Construction"] (Kyodo News, March 25, 2013). == Archaeological Record == * Meiji Period: Designated as Emperor Kaika’s mausoleum by the Imperial Household Agency. * 1975: Excavation during torii reconstruction revealed Edo-period burial items. * 1976: Pre-construction survey of embankments. * 1988: Structural maintenance of protective fencing and gates. * 2002: Investigations during road construction. * 2008: Reconstruction of torii uncovered ossuary vessels, wooden coffins, and human remains. == Mound area == * Total length: Approx. 100 meters * Rear circular mound: ** Diameter: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 8 meters * Front trapezoidal mound: ** Width: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 6 meters == Buried Individual == The actual individual interred at Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Kaika, the 9th emperor of Japan.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Mausolea - Imperial Household Agency] Ancient records, such as the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}* and *{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}*, reference the location as "Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue," supporting this identification. However, Emperor Kaika and other rulers of the {{ill|Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|ja|欠史八代}} are considered mythological or semi-legendary figures.{{Cite book|title=Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum Overview|publisher=平凡社|year=2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dramatic Nara|author=Ogura Tsukiko|publisher=京阪奈情報教育出版|year=2010}} * {{Cite book|title=Guide to Imperial Mausolea and Ancient Tombs|author=Mori Hiroshi|publisher=筑摩書房|year=2011}} * {{Cite book|title=Continuation of Japan’s Ancient Tombs|author=Nishizaki Takuya|publisher=東京堂出版|year=2002}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}} * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|207421770|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum}} * Kofun |map_dot_label Position = bottom |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-SSW }} [[File:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun, haisho-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}'s Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum]] '''Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun''' (念仏寺山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Aburakōji-chō|ja|油阪町}}, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "'''Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum'''" (春日率川坂上陵) and attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, the 9th emperor of Japan. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tumulus is located in central Nara City, between {{ill|Sanjo Street|ja|三条通り}} and Aburakōji Street.{{Cite book|title=Nara City Historical Records, Archaeology Volume|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1971|pages=82-85}} It has not undergone extensive excavation due to its designation as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. The tumulus is oriented south-southeast. During the {{ill|Early Modern Period|ja|近世}}, the tumulus fell under the temple grounds of {{ill|Nenbutsuji Temple (Nara City)|ja|念仏寺 (奈良市)}} and was utilized as a cemetery, leading to mound alterations. In the late Edo period, cemetery relocation and restoration of the mausoleum took place.{{Cite book|title=Nara Prefecture Archaeological History|publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|year=2001}} Current features include flattened tops of the circular and trapezoidal sections, and the mound's original stepped construction is no longer evident. === Artifacts and Age === Fragments of cylindrical haniwa discovered on the western embankment suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century (middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}).{{Cite book|title=Historical Atlas of Japan|publisher=平凡社|year=1981}} Excavations in 1975 and 2008 uncovered Edo-period burial artifacts, including ossuary vessels and wooden coffins.[http://www.47news.jp/CN/200812/CN2008121101000797.html "Artifacts from Edo Period Uncovered near Emperor Kaika's Mausoleum"] (Kyodo News, December 11, 2008). During the construction of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, many ancient Kofuns were destroyed. However, Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun and a few others, such as the {{ill|Hōraisan Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳}}, survived.[https://web.archive.org/web/20130615225332/http://www.47news.jp/CN/201303/CN2013032501001891.html "Ancient Tombs Destroyed for Heijō-kyō Construction"] (Kyodo News, March 25, 2013). == Archaeological Record == * Meiji Period: Designated as Emperor Kaika’s mausoleum by the Imperial Household Agency. * 1975: Excavation during torii reconstruction revealed Edo-period burial items. * 1976: Pre-construction survey of embankments. * 1988: Structural maintenance of protective fencing and gates. * 2002: Investigations during road construction. * 2008: Reconstruction of torii uncovered ossuary vessels, wooden coffins, and human remains. == Mound area == * Total length: Approx. 100 meters * Rear circular mound: ** Diameter: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 8 meters * Front trapezoidal mound: ** Width: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 6 meters == Buried Individual == The actual individual interred at Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Kaika, the 9th emperor of Japan.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Mausolea - Imperial Household Agency] Ancient records, such as the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}* and *{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}*, reference the location as "Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue," supporting this identification. However, Emperor Kaika and other rulers of the {{ill|Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|ja|欠史八代}} are considered mythological or semi-legendary figures.{{Cite book|title=Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum Overview|publisher=平凡社|year=2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dramatic Nara|author=Ogura Tsukiko|publisher=京阪奈情報教育出版|year=2010}} * {{Cite book|title=Guide to Imperial Mausolea and Ancient Tombs|author=Mori Hiroshi|publisher=筑摩書房|year=2011}} * {{Cite book|title=Continuation of Japan’s Ancient Tombs|author=Nishizaki Takuya|publisher=東京堂出版|year=2002}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}} * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|207421770|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/009/index.html Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] However, Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun and a few others, such as the {{ill|Hōraisan Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳}}, survived.[https://web.archive.org/web/20130615225332/http://www.47news.jp/CN/201303/CN2013032501001891.html "Ancient Tombs Destroyed for Heijō-kyō Construction"] (Kyodo News, March 25, 2013). == Archaeological Record == * Meiji Period: Designated as Emperor Kaika’s mausoleum by the Imperial Household Agency. * 1975: Excavation during torii reconstruction revealed Edo-period burial items. * 1976: Pre-construction survey of embankments. * 1988: Structural maintenance of protective fencing and gates. * 2002: Investigations during road construction. * 2008: Reconstruction of torii uncovered ossuary vessels, wooden coffins, and human remains. == Mound area == * Total length: Approx. 100 meters * Rear circular mound: ** Diameter: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 8 meters * Front trapezoidal mound: ** Width: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 6 meters == Buried Individual == The actual individual interred at Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun is unknown. Orthographic aerial view of Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun
{{small|{{Geospatial Orthographic map}}}} |alternate_name = Kōbō-yama Kofun / Sakaue-yama Kofun |Cluster = {{ill|Itsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|率川古墳群}}Dramatic Nara, p.151Refer to materials from the Nara City Board of Education, Archaeological Section. |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, Aburakōji-chō |coordinates = 34°41′0.49″N |coordinates = 135°49′26.44″E |ISO = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |map_size = Total mound length approx. 100m
(Estimated restoration: 115-120m)
Height approx. 8m (rear circular mound) |Burial Facility = Unknown |built = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Interred_individual = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, 9th emperor |burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} |Mausoleum = {{ill|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum|ja|春日率川坂上陵}} |Special Notes = |map = Nara city |map_dot_label = Nenbutsuji-yama
Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-SSW }} [[File:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun, haisho-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}'s Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum]] '''Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun''' (念仏寺山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Aburakōji-chō|ja|油阪町}}, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "'''Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum'''" (春日率川坂上陵) and attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, the 9th emperor of Japan. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tumulus is located in central Nara City, between {{ill|Sanjo Street|ja|三条通り}} and Aburakōji Street.{{Cite book|title=Nara City Historical Records, Archaeology Volume|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1971|pages=82-85}} It has not undergone extensive excavation due to its designation as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. The tumulus is oriented south-southeast. During the {{ill|Early Modern Period|ja|近世}}, the tumulus fell under the temple grounds of {{ill|Nenbutsuji Temple (Nara City)|ja|念仏寺 (奈良市)}} and was utilized as a cemetery, leading to mound alterations. In the late Edo period, cemetery relocation and restoration of the mausoleum took place.{{Cite book|title=Nara Prefecture Archaeological History|publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|year=2001}} Current features include flattened tops of the circular and trapezoidal sections, and the mound's original stepped construction is no longer evident. === Artifacts and Age === Fragments of cylindrical haniwa discovered on the western embankment suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century (middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}).{{Cite book|title=Historical Atlas of Japan|publisher=平凡社|year=1981}} Excavations in 1975 and 2008 uncovered Edo-period burial artifacts, including ossuary vessels and wooden coffins.[http://www.47news.jp/CN/200812/CN2008121101000797.html "Artifacts from Edo Period Uncovered near Emperor Kaika's Mausoleum"] (Kyodo News, December 11, 2008). During the construction of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, many ancient Kofuns were destroyed. However, Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun and a few others, such as the {{ill|Hōraisan Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳}}, survived.[https://web.archive.org/web/20130615225332/http://www.47news.jp/CN/201303/CN2013032501001891.html "Ancient Tombs Destroyed for Heijō-kyō Construction"] (Kyodo News, March 25, 2013). == Archaeological Record == * Meiji Period: Designated as Emperor Kaika’s mausoleum by the Imperial Household Agency. * 1975: Excavation during torii reconstruction revealed Edo-period burial items. * 1976: Pre-construction survey of embankments. * 1988: Structural maintenance of protective fencing and gates. * 2002: Investigations during road construction. * 2008: Reconstruction of torii uncovered ossuary vessels, wooden coffins, and human remains. == Mound area == * Total length: Approx. 100 meters * Rear circular mound: ** Diameter: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 8 meters * Front trapezoidal mound: ** Width: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 6 meters == Buried Individual == The actual individual interred at Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Kaika, the 9th emperor of Japan.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Mausolea - Imperial Household Agency] Ancient records, such as the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}* and *{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}*, reference the location as "Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue," supporting this identification. However, Emperor Kaika and other rulers of the {{ill|Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|ja|欠史八代}} are considered mythological or semi-legendary figures.{{Cite book|title=Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum Overview|publisher=平凡社|year=2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dramatic Nara|author=Ogura Tsukiko|publisher=京阪奈情報教育出版|year=2010}} * {{Cite book|title=Guide to Imperial Mausolea and Ancient Tombs|author=Mori Hiroshi|publisher=筑摩書房|year=2011}} * {{Cite book|title=Continuation of Japan’s Ancient Tombs|author=Nishizaki Takuya|publisher=東京堂出版|year=2002}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}} * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|207421770|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum}} * Orthographic aerial view of Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun
{{small|{{Geospatial Orthographic map}}}} |alternate_name = Kōbō-yama Kofun / Sakaue-yama Kofun |Cluster = {{ill|Itsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|率川古墳群}}Dramatic Nara, p.151Refer to materials from the Nara City Board of Education, Archaeological Section. |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, Aburakōji-chō |coordinates = 34°41′0.49″N |coordinates = 135°49′26.44″E |ISO = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-typed tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |map_size = Total mound length approx. 100m
(Estimated restoration: 115-120m)
Height approx. 8m (rear circular mound) |Burial Facility = Unknown |built = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Interred_individual = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, 9th emperor |burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} |Mausoleum = {{ill|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum|ja|春日率川坂上陵}} |notes = |map = Nara city |map_dot_label = Nenbutsuji-yama
Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom |Icon = Keyhole-typed tumulus-SSW }} [[File:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun, haisho-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}'s Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum]] '''Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun''' (念仏寺山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-typed tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Aburakōji-chō|ja|油阪町}}, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "'''Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum'''" (春日率川坂上陵) and attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, the 9th emperor of Japan. == Overview == This large keyhole-typed tumulus is located in central Nara City, between {{ill|Sanjo Street|ja|三条通り}} and Aburakōji Street.{{Cite book|title=Nara City Historical Records, Archaeology Volume|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1971|pages=82-85}} It has not undergone extensive excavation due to its designation as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. The tumulus is oriented south-southeast. During the {{ill|Early Modern Period|ja|近世}}, the tumulus fell under the temple grounds of {{ill|Nenbutsuji Temple (Nara City)|ja|念仏寺 (奈良市)}} and was utilized as a cemetery, leading to mound alterations. In the late Edo period, cemetery relocation and restoration of the mausoleum took place.{{Cite book|title=Nara Prefecture Archaeological History|publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|year=2001}} Current features include flattened tops of the circular and trapezoidal sections, and the mound's original stepped construction is no longer evident. === Artifacts and Age === Fragments of cylindrical haniwa discovered on the western embankment suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century (middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}).{{Cite book|title=Historical Atlas of Japan|publisher=平凡社|year=1981}} Excavations in 1975 and 2008 uncovered Edo-period burial artifacts, including ossuary vessels and wooden coffins.[http://www.47news.jp/CN/200812/CN2008121101000797.html "Artifacts from Edo Period Uncovered near Emperor Kaika's Mausoleum"] (Kyodo News, December 11, 2008). During the construction of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, many ancient Kofuns were destroyed. However, Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun and a few others, such as the {{ill|Hōraisan Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳}}, survived.[https://web.archive.org/web/20130615225332/http://www.47news.jp/CN/201303/CN2013032501001891.html "Ancient Tombs Destroyed for Heijō-kyō Construction"] (Kyodo News, March 25, 2013). == Archaeological Record == * Meiji Period: Designated as Emperor Kaika’s mausoleum by the Imperial Household Agency. * 1975: Excavation during torii reconstruction revealed Edo-period burial items. * 1976: Pre-construction survey of embankments. * 1988: Structural maintenance of protective fencing and gates. * 2002: Investigations during road construction. * 2008: Reconstruction of torii uncovered ossuary vessels, wooden coffins, and human remains. == Mound area == * Total length: Approx. 100 meters * Rear circular mound: ** Diameter: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 8 meters * Front trapezoidal mound: ** Width: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 6 meters == Buried Individual == The actual individual interred at Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Kaika, the 9th emperor of Japan.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Mausolea - Imperial Household Agency] Ancient records, such as the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}* and *{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}*, reference the location as "Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue," supporting this identification. However, Emperor Kaika and other rulers of the {{ill|Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|ja|欠史八代}} are considered mythological or semi-legendary figures.{{Cite book|title=Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum Overview|publisher=平凡社|year=2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dramatic Nara|author=Ogura Tsukiko|publisher=京阪奈情報教育出版|year=2010}} * {{Cite book|title=Guide to Imperial Mausolea and Ancient Tombs|author=Mori Hiroshi|publisher=筑摩書房|year=2011}} * {{Cite book|title=Continuation of Japan’s Ancient Tombs|author=Nishizaki Takuya|publisher=東京堂出版|year=2002}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}} * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|207421770|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum}} * {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[ja:念仏寺山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun (念仏寺山古墳) |image = [[File:Kaika Tennnouryou.png|260px]]
Orthographic aerial view of Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun
{{small|{{Geospatial Orthographic map}}}} |alternate_name = Kōbō-yama Kofun / Sakaue-yama Kofun |Cluster = {{ill|Itsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|率川古墳群}}Dramatic Nara, p.151Refer to materials from the Nara City Board of Education, Archaeological Section. |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, Aburakōji-chō |coordinates = 34°41′0.49″N |coordinates = 135°49′26.44″E |ISO = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |map_size = Total mound length approx. 100m
(Estimated restoration: 115-120m)
Height approx. 8m (rear circular mound) |burial = Unknown |built = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Interred_individual = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, 9th emperor |burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} |Mausoleum = {{ill|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum|ja|春日率川坂上陵}} |notes = |map = Nara city |map_dot_label = Nenbutsuji-yama
Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-SSW }} [[File:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun, haisho-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}'s Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum]] '''Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun''' (念仏寺山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Aburakōji-chō|ja|油阪町}}, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "'''Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum'''" (春日率川坂上陵) and attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, the 9th emperor of Japan. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tumulus is located in central Nara City, between {{ill|Sanjo Street|ja|三条通り}} and Aburakōji Street.{{Cite book|title=Nara City Historical Records, Archaeology Volume|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1971|pages=82-85}} It has not undergone extensive excavation due to its designation as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. The tumulus is oriented south-southeast. During the {{ill|Early Modern Period|ja|近世}}, the tumulus fell under the temple grounds of {{ill|Nenbutsuji Temple (Nara City)|ja|念仏寺 (奈良市)}} and was utilized as a cemetery, leading to mound alterations. In the late Edo period, cemetery relocation and restoration of the mausoleum took place.{{Cite book|title=Nara Prefecture Archaeological History|publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|year=2001}} Current features include flattened tops of the circular and trapezoidal sections, and the mound's original stepped construction is no longer evident. === Artifacts and Age === Fragments of cylindrical haniwa discovered on the western embankment suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century (middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}).{{Cite book|title=Historical Atlas of Japan|publisher=平凡社|year=1981}} Excavations in 1975 and 2008 uncovered Edo-period burial artifacts, including ossuary vessels and wooden coffins.[http://www.47news.jp/CN/200812/CN2008121101000797.html "Artifacts from Edo Period Uncovered near Emperor Kaika's Mausoleum"] (Kyodo News, December 11, 2008). During the construction of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, many ancient Kofuns were destroyed. However, Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun and a few others, such as the {{ill|Hōraisan Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳}}, survived.[https://web.archive.org/web/20130615225332/http://www.47news.jp/CN/201303/CN2013032501001891.html "Ancient Tombs Destroyed for Heijō-kyō Construction"] (Kyodo News, March 25, 2013). == Archaeological Record == * Meiji Period: Designated as Emperor Kaika’s mausoleum by the Imperial Household Agency. * 1975: Excavation during torii reconstruction revealed Edo-period burial items. * 1976: Pre-construction survey of embankments. * 1988: Structural maintenance of protective fencing and gates. * 2002: Investigations during road construction. * 2008: Reconstruction of torii uncovered ossuary vessels, wooden coffins, and human remains. == Mound area == * Total length: Approx. 100 meters * Rear circular mound: ** Diameter: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 8 meters * Front trapezoidal mound: ** Width: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 6 meters == Buried Individual == The actual individual interred at Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Kaika, the 9th emperor of Japan.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Mausolea - Imperial Household Agency] Ancient records, such as the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}* and *{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}*, reference the location as "Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue," supporting this identification. However, Emperor Kaika and other rulers of the {{ill|Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|ja|欠史八代}} are considered mythological or semi-legendary figures.{{Cite book|title=Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum Overview|publisher=平凡社|year=2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dramatic Nara|author=Ogura Tsukiko|publisher=京阪奈情報教育出版|year=2010}} * {{Cite book|title=Guide to Imperial Mausolea and Ancient Tombs|author=Mori Hiroshi|publisher=筑摩書房|year=2011}} * {{Cite book|title=Continuation of Japan’s Ancient Tombs|author=Nishizaki Takuya|publisher=東京堂出版|year=2002}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}} * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|207421770|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/009/index.html Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] Orthographic aerial view of Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun
{{small|{{Geospatial Orthographic map}}}} |alternate_name = Kōbō-yama Kofun / Sakaue-yama Kofun |Cluster = {{ill|Itsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|率川古墳群}}Dramatic Nara, p.151Refer to materials from the Nara City Board of Education, Archaeological Section. |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, Aburakōji-chō |coordinates = 34°41′0.49″N |coordinates = 135°49′26.44″E |ISO = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |map_size = Total mound length approx. 100m
(Estimated restoration: 115-120m)
Height approx. 8m (rear circular mound) |burial = Unknown |built = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Interred_individual = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, 9th emperor |burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} |Mausoleum = {{ill|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum|ja|春日率川坂上陵}} |notes = |map = Nara city |map_dot_label = Nenbutsuji-yama
Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom |Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-SSW }} [[File:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun, haisho-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}'s Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum]] '''Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun''' (念仏寺山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Aburakōji-chō|ja|油阪町}}, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "'''Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum'''" (春日率川坂上陵) and attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, the 9th emperor of Japan. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tumulus is located in central Nara City, between {{ill|Sanjo Street|ja|三条通り}} and Aburakōji Street.{{Cite book|title=Nara City Historical Records, Archaeology Volume|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1971|pages=82-85}} It has not undergone extensive excavation due to its designation as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. The tumulus is oriented south-southeast. During the {{ill|Early Modern Period|ja|近世}}, the tumulus fell under the temple grounds of {{ill|Nenbutsuji Temple (Nara City)|ja|念仏寺 (奈良市)}} and was utilized as a cemetery, leading to mound alterations. In the late Edo period, cemetery relocation and restoration of the mausoleum took place.{{Cite book|title=Nara Prefecture Archaeological History|publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|year=2001}} Current features include flattened tops of the circular and trapezoidal sections, and the mound's original stepped construction is no longer evident. === Artifacts and Age === Fragments of cylindrical haniwa discovered on the western embankment suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century (middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}).{{Cite book|title=Historical Atlas of Japan|publisher=平凡社|year=1981}} Excavations in 1975 and 2008 uncovered Edo-period burial artifacts, including ossuary vessels and wooden coffins.[http://www.47news.jp/CN/200812/CN2008121101000797.html "Artifacts from Edo Period Uncovered near Emperor Kaika's Mausoleum"] (Kyodo News, December 11, 2008). During the construction of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, many ancient Kofuns were destroyed. However, Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun and a few others, such as the {{ill|Hōraisan Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳}}, survived.[https://web.archive.org/web/20130615225332/http://www.47news.jp/CN/201303/CN2013032501001891.html "Ancient Tombs Destroyed for Heijō-kyō Construction"] (Kyodo News, March 25, 2013). == Archaeological Record == * Meiji Period: Designated as Emperor Kaika’s mausoleum by the Imperial Household Agency. * 1975: Excavation during torii reconstruction revealed Edo-period burial items. * 1976: Pre-construction survey of embankments. * 1988: Structural maintenance of protective fencing and gates. * 2002: Investigations during road construction. * 2008: Reconstruction of torii uncovered ossuary vessels, wooden coffins, and human remains. == Mound area == * Total length: Approx. 100 meters * Rear circular mound: ** Diameter: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 8 meters * Front trapezoidal mound: ** Width: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 6 meters == Buried Individual == The actual individual interred at Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Kaika, the 9th emperor of Japan.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Mausolea - Imperial Household Agency] Ancient records, such as the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}* and *{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}*, reference the location as "Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue," supporting this identification. However, Emperor Kaika and other rulers of the {{ill|Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|ja|欠史八代}} are considered mythological or semi-legendary figures.{{Cite book|title=Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum Overview|publisher=平凡社|year=2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dramatic Nara|author=Ogura Tsukiko|publisher=京阪奈情報教育出版|year=2010}} * {{Cite book|title=Guide to Imperial Mausolea and Ancient Tombs|author=Mori Hiroshi|publisher=筑摩書房|year=2011}} * {{Cite book|title=Continuation of Japan’s Ancient Tombs|author=Nishizaki Takuya|publisher=東京堂出版|year=2002}} == In the late Edo period, cemetery relocation and restoration of the mausoleum took place.{{Cite book|title=Nara Prefecture Archaeological History|publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|year=2001}} Current features include flattened tops of the circular and trapezoidal sections, and the mound's original stepped construction is no longer evident. === Artifacts and Age === Fragments of cylindrical haniwa discovered on the western embankment suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century (middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}).{{Cite book|title=Historical Atlas of Japan|publisher=平凡社|year=1981}} Excavations in 1975 and 2008 uncovered Edo-period burial artifacts, including ossuary vessels and wooden coffins.[http://www.47news.jp/CN/200812/CN2008121101000797.html "Artifacts from Edo Period Uncovered near Emperor Kaika's Mausoleum"] (Kyodo News, December 11, 2008). During the construction of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, many ancient Kofuns were destroyed. However, Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun and a few others, such as the {{ill|Hōraisan Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳}}, survived.[https://web.archive.org/web/20130615225332/http://www.47news.jp/CN/201303/CN2013032501001891.html "Ancient Tombs Destroyed for Heijō-kyō Construction"] (Kyodo News, March 25, 2013). == Archaeological Record == * Meiji Period: Designated as Emperor Kaika’s mausoleum by the Imperial Household Agency. * 1975: Excavation during torii reconstruction revealed Edo-period burial items. * 1976: Pre-construction survey of embankments. * 1988: Structural maintenance of protective fencing and gates. * 2002: Investigations during road construction. * 2008: Reconstruction of torii uncovered ossuary vessels, wooden coffins, and human remains. == Mound area == * Total length: Approx. 100 meters * Rear circular mound: ** Diameter: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 8 meters * Front trapezoidal mound: ** Width: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 6 meters == Buried Individual == The actual individual interred at Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Kaika, the 9th emperor of Japan.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Mausolea - Imperial Household Agency] Ancient records, such as the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}* and *{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}*, reference the location as "Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue," supporting this identification. However, Emperor Kaika and other rulers of the {{ill|Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|ja|欠史八代}} are considered mythological or semi-legendary figures.{{Cite book|title=Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum Overview|publisher=平凡社|year=2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dramatic Nara|author=Ogura Tsukiko|publisher=京阪奈情報教育出版|year=2010}} * {{Cite book|title=Guide to Imperial Mausolea and Ancient Tombs|author=Mori Hiroshi|publisher=筑摩書房|year=2011}} * {{Cite book|title=Continuation of Japan’s Ancient Tombs|author=Nishizaki Takuya|publisher=東京堂出版|year=2002}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}} * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|207421770|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/009/index.html Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[ja:念仏寺山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun (念仏寺山古墳) |image = [[File:Kaika Tennnouryou.png|260px]]
Orthographic aerial view of Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun
{{small|{{Geospatial Orthographic map}}}} |alternate_name = Kōbō-yama Kofun / Sakaue-yama Kofun |Cluster = {{ill|Itsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|率川古墳群}}Dramatic Nara, p.151Refer to materials from the Nara City Board of Education, Archaeological Section. |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, Aburakōji-chō |coordinates = 34°41′0.49″N |coordinates = 135°49′26.44″E |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |map_size = Total mound length approx. 100m
(Estimated restoration: 115-120m)
Height approx. 8m (rear circular mound) |burial = Unknown |built = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Interred_individual = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, 9th emperor |burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} |Mausoleum = {{ill|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum|ja|春日率川坂上陵}} |notes = |map = Nara city |map_dot_label = Nenbutsuji-yama
Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-SSW }} [[File:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun, haisho-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}'s Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum]] '''Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun''' (念仏寺山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Aburakōji-chō|ja|油阪町}}, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "'''Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum'''" (春日率川坂上陵) and attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, the 9th emperor of Japan. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tumulus is located in central Nara City, between {{ill|Sanjo Street|ja|三条通り}} and Aburakōji Street.{{Cite book|title=Nara City Historical Records, Archaeology Volume|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1971|pages=82-85}} It has not undergone extensive excavation due to its designation as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. The tumulus is oriented south-southeast. During the {{ill|Early Modern Period|ja|近世}}, the tumulus fell under the temple grounds of {{ill|Nenbutsuji Temple (Nara City)|ja|念仏寺 (奈良市)}} and was utilized as a cemetery, leading to mound alterations. In the late Edo period, cemetery relocation and restoration of the mausoleum took place.{{Cite book|title=Nara Prefecture Archaeological History|publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|year=2001}} Current features include flattened tops of the circular and trapezoidal sections, and the mound's original stepped construction is no longer evident. === Artifacts and Age === Fragments of cylindrical haniwa discovered on the western embankment suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century (middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}).{{Cite book|title=Historical Atlas of Japan|publisher=平凡社|year=1981}} Excavations in 1975 and 2008 uncovered Edo-period burial artifacts, including ossuary vessels and wooden coffins.[http://www.47news.jp/CN/200812/CN2008121101000797.html "Artifacts from Edo Period Uncovered near Emperor Kaika's Mausoleum"] (Kyodo News, December 11, 2008). During the construction of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, many ancient Kofuns were destroyed. However, Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun and a few others, such as the {{ill|Hōraisan Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳}}, survived.[https://web.archive.org/web/20130615225332/http://www.47news.jp/CN/201303/CN2013032501001891.html "Ancient Tombs Destroyed for Heijō-kyō Construction"] (Kyodo News, March 25, 2013). == Archaeological Record == * Meiji Period: Designated as Emperor Kaika’s mausoleum by the Imperial Household Agency. * 1975: Excavation during torii reconstruction revealed Edo-period burial items. * 1976: Pre-construction survey of embankments. * 1988: Structural maintenance of protective fencing and gates. * 2002: Investigations during road construction. * 2008: Reconstruction of torii uncovered ossuary vessels, wooden coffins, and human remains. == Mound area == * Total length: Approx. 100 meters * Rear circular mound: ** Diameter: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 8 meters * Front trapezoidal mound: ** Width: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 6 meters == Buried Individual == The actual individual interred at Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Kaika, the 9th emperor of Japan.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Mausolea - Imperial Household Agency] Ancient records, such as the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}* and *{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}*, reference the location as "Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue," supporting this identification. However, Emperor Kaika and other rulers of the {{ill|Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|ja|欠史八代}} are considered mythological or semi-legendary figures.{{Cite book|title=Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum Overview|publisher=平凡社|year=2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dramatic Nara|author=Ogura Tsukiko|publisher=京阪奈情報教育出版|year=2010}} * {{Cite book|title=Guide to Imperial Mausolea and Ancient Tombs|author=Mori Hiroshi|publisher=筑摩書房|year=2011}} * {{Cite book|title=Continuation of Japan’s Ancient Tombs|author=Nishizaki Takuya|publisher=東京堂出版|year=2002}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}} * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|207421770|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/009/index.html Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] {{small|{{Geospatial Orthographic map}}}} |alternate_name = Kōbō-yama Kofun / Sakaue-yama Kofun |Cluster = {{ill|Itsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|率川古墳群}}Dramatic Nara, p.151Refer to materials from the Nara City Board of Education, Archaeological Section. |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, Aburakōji-chō |coordinates = 34°41′0.49″N |coordinates = 135°49′26.44″E |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |map_size = Total mound length approx. 100m
