However, Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun and a few others, such as the {{ill|Hōraisan Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, survived.[https://web.archive.org/web/20130615225332/http://www.47news.jp/CN/201303/CN2013032501001891.html "Ancient Tombs Destroyed for Heijō-kyō built"] (Kyodo News, March 25, 2013). == Archaeological Record == * Meiji built: Designated as Emperor Kaika’s mausoleum by the Imperial Household Agency. * 1975: Excavation during torii reconstruction revealed Edo-period burial items. * 1976: Pre-construction survey of embankments. * 1988: Structural maintenance of protective fencing and gates. * 2002: Investigations during road construction. * 2008: Reconstruction of torii uncovered ossuary vessels, wooden coffins, and human remains. == Mound area == * Total length: Approx. 100 meters * Rear circular mound: ** Diameter: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 8 meters * Front trapezoidal mound: ** Width: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 6 meters == Interred_individual == The actual individual interred at Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Kaika, the 9th emperor of Japan.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Mausolea - Imperial Household Agency] Ancient records, such as the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}* and *{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}*, reference the location as "Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue," supporting this identification. However, Emperor Kaika and other rulers of the {{ill|Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|ja|欠史八代|12=draft|12=draft}} are considered mythological or semi-legendary figures.{{Cite book|title=Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum Overview|publisher=平凡社|year=2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dramatic Nara|author=Ogura Tsukiko|publisher=京阪奈情報教育出版|year=2010}} * {{Cite book|title=Guide to Imperial Mausolea and Ancient Tombs|author=Mori Hiroshi|publisher=筑摩書房|year=2011}} * {{Cite book|title=Continuation of Japan’s Ancient Tombs|author=Nishizaki Takuya|publisher=東京堂出版|year=2002}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|207421770|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/009/index.html Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]] [[Category:Nara Prefecture’s Kofun]] [[Category:Nara City history]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]] [[ja:念仏寺山古墳]] [[sn:enbutsuji-yama Kofun]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun (念仏寺山古墳) |image = [[File:Kaika Tennnouryou.png|260px]]
Orthographic aerial view of Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun
{{small|{{Geospatial Orthographic map}}}} |alternate_name = Kōbō-yama Kofun / Sakaue-yama Kofun |Cluster = {{ill|Itsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|率川古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}Dramatic Nara, p.151Refer to materials from the Nara City Board of Education, Archaeological Section. |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Aburakōji-chō |coordinates = 34°41′0.49″N |coordinates = 135°49′26.44″E |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} |map_size = Total mound length approx. 100m
(Estimated restoration: 115-120m)
Height approx. 8m (rear circular mound) |burial = Unknown |built = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} |Interred_individual = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}}) {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, 9th emperor |burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}} |Mausoleum = {{ill|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum|ja|春日率川坂上陵|12=draft|12=draft}} |notes = |map = Nara city |map_dot_label = Nenbutsuji-yama
Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-SSW }} [[File:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun, haisho-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}'s Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum]] '''Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun''' (念仏寺山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Aburakōji-chō|ja|油阪町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is Historic Site1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} as the "'''Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum'''" (春日率川坂上陵) and attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, the 9th emperor of Japan. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tumulus is located in central Nara City, between {{ill|Sanjo Street|ja|三条通り|12=draft|12=draft}} and Aburakōji Street.{{Cite book|title=Nara City Historical Records, Archaeology Volume|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1971|pages=82-85}} It has not undergone extensive excavation due to its Historic Site as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}}. The tumulus is oriented south-southeast. During the {{ill|Early Modern built|ja|近世|12=draft|12=draft}}, the tumulus fell under the temple grounds of {{ill|Nenbutsuji Temple (Nara City)|ja|念仏寺 (奈良市)|12=draft|12=draft}} and was utilized as a cemetery, leading to mound alterations. In the late Edo period, cemetery relocation and restoration of the mausoleum took place.{{Cite book|title=Nara Prefecture Archaeological History|publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|year=2001}} Current features include flattened tops of the circular and trapezoidal sections, and the mound's original stepped construction is no longer evident. === burial goods and Age === Fragments of cylindrical haniwa discovered on the western embankment suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century (middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}).{{Cite book|title=Historical Atlas of Japan|publisher=平凡社|year=1981}} Excavations in 1975 and 2008 uncovered Edo-period burial burial goods, including ossuary vessels and wooden coffins.[http://www.47news.jp/CN/200812/CN2008121101000797.html "burial goods from Edo built Uncovered near Emperor Kaika's Mausoleum"] (Kyodo News, December 11, 2008). During the construction of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京|12=draft|12=draft}}, many ancient Kofuns were destroyed. However, Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun and a few others, such as the {{ill|Hōraisan Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, survived.[https://web.archive.org/web/20130615225332/http://www.47news.jp/CN/201303/CN2013032501001891.html "Ancient Tombs Destroyed for Heijō-kyō built"] (Kyodo News, March 25, 2013). == Archaeological Record == * Meiji built: Designated as Emperor Kaika’s mausoleum by the Imperial Household Agency. * 1975: Excavation during torii reconstruction revealed Edo-period burial items. * 1976: Pre-construction survey of embankments. * 1988: Structural maintenance of protective fencing and gates. * 2002: Investigations during road construction. * 2008: Reconstruction of torii uncovered ossuary vessels, wooden coffins, and human remains. == Mound area == * Total length: Approx. 100 meters * Rear circular mound: ** Diameter: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 8 meters * Front trapezoidal mound: ** Width: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 6 meters == Interred_individual == The actual individual interred at Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Kaika, the 9th emperor of Japan.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Mausolea - Imperial Household Agency] Ancient records, such as the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}* and *{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}*, reference the location as "Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue," supporting this identification. However, Emperor Kaika and other rulers of the {{ill|Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|ja|欠史八代|12=draft|12=draft}} are considered mythological or semi-legendary figures.{{Cite book|title=Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum Overview|publisher=平凡社|year=2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dramatic Nara|author=Ogura Tsukiko|publisher=京阪奈情報教育出版|year=2010}} * {{Cite book|title=Guide to Imperial Mausolea and Ancient Tombs|author=Mori Hiroshi|publisher=筑摩書房|year=2011}} * {{Cite book|title=Continuation of Japan’s Ancient Tombs|author=Nishizaki Takuya|publisher=東京堂出版|year=2002}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|207421770|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/009/index.html Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]] [[Category:Nara Prefecture’s Kofun]] [[Category:Nara City history]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]] [[ja:念仏寺山古墳]] [[sn:enbutsuji-yama Kofun]]

{{small|{{Geospatial Orthographic map}}}} |alternate_name = Kōbō-yama Kofun / Sakaue-yama Kofun |Cluster = {{ill|Itsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|率川古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}Dramatic Nara, p.151Refer to materials from the Nara City Board of Education, Archaeological Section. |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Aburakōji-chō |coordinates = 34°41′0.49″N |coordinates = 135°49′26.44″E |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} |map_size = Total mound length approx. 100m
(Estimated restoration: 115-120m)
Height approx. 8m (rear circular mound) |burial = Unknown |built = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} |Interred_individual = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}}) {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, 9th emperor |burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}} |Mausoleum = {{ill|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum|ja|春日率川坂上陵|12=draft|12=draft}} |notes = |map = Nara city |map_dot_label = Nenbutsuji-yama
Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-SSW }} [[File:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun, haisho-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}'s Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum]] '''Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun''' (念仏寺山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Aburakōji-chō|ja|油阪町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is Historic Site1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} as the "'''Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum'''" (春日率川坂上陵) and attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, the 9th emperor of Japan. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tumulus is located in central Nara City, between {{ill|Sanjo Street|ja|三条通り|12=draft|12=draft}} and Aburakōji Street.{{Cite book|title=Nara City Historical Records, Archaeology Volume|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1971|pages=82-85}} It has not undergone extensive excavation due to its Historic Site as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}}. The tumulus is oriented south-southeast. During the {{ill|Early Modern built|ja|近世|12=draft|12=draft}}, the tumulus fell under the temple grounds of {{ill|Nenbutsuji Temple (Nara City)|ja|念仏寺 (奈良市)|12=draft|12=draft}} and was utilized as a cemetery, leading to mound alterations. In the late Edo period, cemetery relocation and restoration of the mausoleum took place.{{Cite book|title=Nara Prefecture Archaeological History|publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|year=2001}} Current features include flattened tops of the circular and trapezoidal sections, and the mound's original stepped construction is no longer evident. === burial goods and Age === Fragments of cylindrical haniwa discovered on the western embankment suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century (middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}).{{Cite book|title=Historical Atlas of Japan|publisher=平凡社|year=1981}} Excavations in 1975 and 2008 uncovered Edo-period burial burial goods, including ossuary vessels and wooden coffins.[http://www.47news.jp/CN/200812/CN2008121101000797.html "burial goods from Edo built Uncovered near Emperor Kaika's Mausoleum"] (Kyodo News, December 11, 2008). During the construction of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京|12=draft|12=draft}}, many ancient Kofuns were destroyed. However, Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun and a few others, such as the {{ill|Hōraisan Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|1=Hōraiyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, survived.[https://web.archive.org/web/20130615225332/http://www.47news.jp/CN/201303/CN2013032501001891.html "Ancient Tombs Destroyed for Heijō-kyō built"] (Kyodo News, March 25, 2013). == Archaeological Record == * Meiji built: Designated as Emperor Kaika’s mausoleum by the Imperial Household Agency. * 1975: Excavation during torii reconstruction revealed Edo-period burial items. * 1976: Pre-construction survey of embankments. * 1988: Structural maintenance of protective fencing and gates. * 2002: Investigations during road construction. * 2008: Reconstruction of torii uncovered ossuary vessels, wooden coffins, and human remains. == Mound area == * Total length: Approx. 100 meters * Rear circular mound: ** Diameter: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 8 meters * Front trapezoidal mound: ** Width: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 6 meters == Interred_individual == The actual individual interred at Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Kaika, the 9th emperor of Japan.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Mausolea - Imperial Household Agency] Ancient records, such as the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}* and *{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}*, reference the location as "Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue," supporting this identification. However, Emperor Kaika and other rulers of the {{ill|Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|ja|欠史八代|12=draft|12=draft}} are considered mythological or semi-legendary figures.{{Cite book|title=Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum Overview|publisher=平凡社|year=2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dramatic Nara|author=Ogura Tsukiko|publisher=京阪奈情報教育出版|year=2010}} * {{Cite book|title=Guide to Imperial Mausolea and Ancient Tombs|author=Mori Hiroshi|publisher=筑摩書房|year=2011}} * {{Cite book|title=Continuation of Japan’s Ancient Tombs|author=Nishizaki Takuya|publisher=東京堂出版|year=2002}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|207421770|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/009/index.html Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]] [[Category:Nara Prefecture’s Kofun]] [[Category:Nara City history]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]] [[ja:念仏寺山古墳]] [[sn:enbutsuji-yama Kofun]]

The tumulus is oriented south-southeast. In the late Edo period, cemetery relocation and restoration of the mausoleum took place.{{Cite book|title=Nara Prefecture Archaeological History|publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|year=2001}} Current features include flattened tops of the circular and trapezoidal sections, and the mound's original stepped construction is no longer evident. === burial goods and Age === Fragments of cylindrical haniwa discovered on the western embankment suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century (middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}).{{Cite book|title=Historical Atlas of Japan|publisher=平凡社|year=1981}} Excavations in 1975 and 2008 uncovered Edo-period burial burial goods, including ossuary vessels and wooden coffins.[http://www.47news.jp/CN/200812/CN2008121101000797.html "burial goods from Edo built Uncovered near Emperor Kaika's Mausoleum"] (Kyodo News, December 11, 2008). During the construction of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, many ancient Kofuns were destroyed. However, Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun and a few others, such as the {{ill|Hōraisan Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|1=Hōraiyama Kofun}}, survived.[https://web.archive.org/web/20130615225332/http://www.47news.jp/CN/201303/CN2013032501001891.html "Ancient Tombs Destroyed for Heijō-kyō built"] (Kyodo News, March 25, 2013). == Archaeological Record == * Meiji built: Designated as Emperor Kaika’s mausoleum by the Imperial Household Agency. * 1975: Excavation during torii reconstruction revealed Edo-period burial items. * 1976: Pre-construction survey of embankments. * 1988: Structural maintenance of protective fencing and gates. * 2002: Investigations during road construction. * 2008: Reconstruction of torii uncovered ossuary vessels, wooden coffins, and human remains. == Mound area == * Total length: Approx. 100 meters * Rear circular mound: ** Diameter: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 8 meters * Front trapezoidal mound: ** Width: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 6 meters == Interred_individual == The actual individual interred at Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun is unknown. revision.comment: 27 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:念仏寺山古墳 up to rev 99995271
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 念仏寺山古墳 |画像 = [[File: Kaika Tennnouryou.png|260px]]
念仏寺山古墳のオルソ空中写真
{{small|{{地理院地図オルソ}}}} |別名 = 弘法山古墳/坂上山古墳 |所属 = 率川古墳群ドラマチック奈良 p.151奈良市教育委員会・埋蔵文化財課資料参照 |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[奈良市]]油阪町字山ノ寺 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 41|緯度秒 = 0.49 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 49|経度秒 = 26.44 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長約100m
（推定復原115-120m?）
高さ約8m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 不明 |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）第9代[[開化天皇]] |出土品 = [[円筒埴輪]]片 |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「春日率川坂上陵」 |特記事項 = |地図 = Nara city |ラベル = 念仏寺山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom |アイコン = 前方後円墳-南南東 }} [[File:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun, haisho-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[開化天皇]]春日率川坂上陵 拝所}}]] '''念仏寺山古墳'''（ねんぶつじやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]油阪町字山ノ寺にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。 {{panoViewer|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|開化天皇春日率川坂上陵 拝所の全天球画像}} 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''春日率川坂上陵'''（かすがのいざかわのさかのえのみささぎ）」として第9代[[開化天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されている。名称は「弘法山古墳（高坊山古墳）」や「坂上山古墳（さかがみやまこふん）」などとも{{Sfn|坂上山古墳（大和前方後円墳集成）|2001}}{{Sfn|春日率河坂上陵（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 == 概要 == [[奈良市]]中心部、[[三条通り]]・油阪通りの間に位置する大型前方後円墳である{{Sfn|春日率河坂上陵（平凡社）|1981}}。[[宮内庁]]により天皇陵に治定されているため、これまでに本格的な調査はなされていない。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を南南東方に向ける{{Sfn|春日率河坂上陵（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。古墳域は[[近世]]頃には近隣の[[念仏寺 (奈良市)|念仏寺]]の寺域とされ、念仏寺の墓地として利用された際に墳丘が削られたが、幕末に墓地の移転と陵の修補がなされた{{Sfn|春日率川坂上陵（国史）}}。ただし、その陵墓修築で墳丘は大規模な改変を受けたと見られている{{Sfn|坂上山古墳（大和前方後円墳集成）|2001}}{{Sfn|春日率河坂上陵（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。現在段築は見られず、後円部・前方部境も不明瞭で、後円部・前方部にかけての墳頂も平坦となっている{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010|pp=92-99}}。埋葬施設は明らかでない{{Sfn|開化天皇陵古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 この念仏寺山古墳は、西側外堤において出土した[[円筒埴輪]]片等から、[[5世紀]]前半（[[古墳時代]]中期）の築造と推定されている{{Sfn|春日率河坂上陵（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。また[[1975年]]（[[昭和]]50年）や[[2008年]]度（[[平成]]20年度）の鳥居建替の際には、江戸時代の墓地化に関連した蔵骨器・木棺が出土している{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010|pp=92-99}}[http://www.47news.jp/CN/200812/CN2008121101000797.html "開化陵周辺に江戸期の墓　宮内庁、研究者に初公開"]（共同通信、2008年12月11日記事（47NEWS））。。 なお古墳域は、後世に営まれた[[平城京]]では左京三条六坊四坪に位置する{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010|pp=92-99}}。平城京造営以前には京域内に広範囲に古墳が分布していたが、平城京造営に際して念仏寺山古墳や[[宝来山古墳]]（[[垂仁天皇]]陵）のほかは多くが破壊・削平を受けたことが知られ[https://web.archive.org/web/20130615225332/http://www.47news.jp/CN/201303/CN2013032501001891.html "平城京に壊された古墳群　大王支えた首長墓か"]（共同通信、2013年3月25日記事（47NEWS））。、本古墳はそれら消滅古墳とともに古墳群を形成したものと考えられている{{Sfn|開化天皇陵古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[明治]]期、宮内省（現・[[宮内庁]]）により開化天皇の陵に治定。 * [[1975年]]（[[昭和]]50年）、鳥居建替工事に伴う立会調査。江戸時代の蔵骨器・木棺などの出土{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010|pp=92-99}}。 * [[1976年]]（昭和51年）、外堤・渡堤工事に伴う事前調査{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010|pp=92-99}}。 * [[1988年]]（昭和63年）、透塀控柱改修工事に伴う立会調査{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010|pp=92-99}}。 * [[2002年]]（[[平成]]14年）、進入路設置その他工事に伴う立会調査{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010|pp=92-99}}。 * [[2008年]]度（平成20年度）、鳥居改築工事に伴う立会調査。蔵骨器・木棺・人骨などの出土{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010|pp=92-99}}。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|角川地名|1990|p=309}}。 * 墳丘長：約100メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：約48メートル ** 高さ：約8メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：約48メートル ** 高さ：約6メートル なお、文久の修陵に伴って墳丘規模は変化したとして、元々の墳丘長を115-120メートル程度と推測する説がある{{Sfn|開化天皇陵古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 == 被葬者 == 念仏寺山古墳の実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]では第9代'''[[開化天皇]]'''の陵に治定している[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html 天皇陵]（宮内庁）。宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/8}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）8コマ。{{Sfn|春日率川坂上陵（国史）}}。開化天皇の陵について、『[[日本書紀]]』では「春日率川坂本陵（坂上陵）」、『古事記』では「伊邪河之坂上」の所在とあるほか、『[[延喜式]]』諸陵寮では「春日率川坂上陵」として兆域は東西5段・南北5段、在京戸10烟を毎年あてる旨とともに遠陵とされ、これらが本古墳に比定されている{{Sfn|春日率川坂上陵（国史）}}。ただし[[綏靖天皇]]（第2代）から開化天皇（第9代）までの「[[欠史八代]]」と称される8代の天皇は、『日本書紀』『古事記』の諸人物の中でも極めて創作性が強い人物になる（詳細は「[[欠史八代]]」を参照）。 なお文久の修陵以前は、後円部墳頂の円丘状の高まり部分のみが開化天皇陵と見なされていたという{{Sfn|開化天皇陵古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1971|chapter=|title=奈良市史 考古編|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|pages=82-85|ref={{Harvid|奈良市史 考古編|1971}}}} * 宮内庁発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=開化天皇 春日率川坂上陵鳥居改築工事に伴う立会調査|title={{PDFlink|[https://shoryobu.kunaicho.go.jp/Publication/PDF/000/kiyo061b032.pdf 書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号]}}|publisher=[[宮内庁書陵部]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010}}}} - リンクは宮内庁「書陵部所蔵資料目録・画像公開システム」。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref=}} *** {{Wikicite|reference=[[関晃]]「開化天皇」|ref={{Harvid|開化天皇（国史）}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=中村一郎「春日率川坂上陵」（開化天皇項目内）|ref={{Harvid|春日率川坂上陵（国史）}}}}。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=春日率河坂上陵|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|春日率河坂上陵（平凡社）|1981}}}} *** {{Wikicite|reference=刊行後版（ジャパンナレッジ収録）、2006年|ref={{Harvid|春日率河坂上陵（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}}}。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=|title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 29 奈良県|publisher=[[角川書店]]|isbn=4040012909|ref={{Harvid|角川地名|1990}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]編|author=鐘方正樹|year=2001|chapter=坂上山古墳|title=大和前方後円墳集成|publisher=[[学生社]]|isbn=4311303270|ref={{Harvid|坂上山古墳（大和前方後円墳集成）|2001}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=西崎卓哉|year=2002|chapter=開化天皇陵古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=978-4490105995|ref={{Harvid|開化天皇陵古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} * その他 ** 小倉つき子 『ドラマチック奈良』 京阪奈情報教育出版、2010年。 == 関連項目 == * [[開化天皇]] * [[率川神社]] - 開化天皇の春日率川宮（春日之伊邪河宮）伝承地。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|207421770|春日率川坂上陵}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/009/index.html 春日率川坂上陵] - 宮内庁 {{DEFAULTSORT:ねんふつしやまこふん}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:奈良市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[念仏寺山古墳]] to [[Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 念仏寺山古墳 |画像 = [[File: Kaika Tennnouryou.png|260px]]
念仏寺山古墳のオルソ空中写真
{{small|{{地理院地図オルソ}}}} |別名 = 弘法山古墳/坂上山古墳 |所属 = 率川古墳群ドラマチック奈良 p.151奈良市教育委員会・埋蔵文化財課資料参照 |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[奈良市]]油阪町字山ノ寺 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 41|緯度秒 = 0.49 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 49|経度秒 = 26.44 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長約100m
（推定復原115-120m?）
高さ約8m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 不明 |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）第9代[[開化天皇]] |出土品 = [[円筒埴輪]]片 |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「春日率川坂上陵」 |特記事項 = |地図 = Nara city |ラベル = 念仏寺山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom |アイコン = 前方後円墳-南南東 }} [[File:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun, haisho-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[開化天皇]]春日率川坂上陵 拝所}}]] '''念仏寺山古墳'''（ねんぶつじやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]油阪町字山ノ寺にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。 {{panoViewer|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|開化天皇春日率川坂上陵 拝所の全天球画像}} 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''春日率川坂上陵'''（かすがのいざかわのさかのえのみささぎ）」として第9代[[開化天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されている。名称は「弘法山古墳（高坊山古墳）」や「坂上山古墳（さかがみやまこふん）」などとも{{Sfn|坂上山古墳（大和前方後円墳集成）|2001}}{{Sfn|春日率河坂上陵（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 == 概要 == [[奈良市]]中心部、[[三条通り]]・油阪通りの間に位置する大型前方後円墳である{{Sfn|春日率河坂上陵（平凡社）|1981}}。[[宮内庁]]により天皇陵に治定されているため、これまでに本格的な調査はなされていない。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を南南東方に向ける{{Sfn|春日率河坂上陵（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。古墳域は[[近世]]頃には近隣の[[念仏寺 (奈良市)|念仏寺]]の寺域とされ、念仏寺の墓地として利用された際に墳丘が削られたが、幕末に墓地の移転と陵の修補がなされた{{Sfn|春日率川坂上陵（国史）}}。ただし、その陵墓修築で墳丘は大規模な改変を受けたと見られている{{Sfn|坂上山古墳（大和前方後円墳集成）|2001}}{{Sfn|春日率河坂上陵（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。現在段築は見られず、後円部・前方部境も不明瞭で、後円部・前方部にかけての墳頂も平坦となっている{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010|pp=92-99}}。埋葬施設は明らかでない{{Sfn|開化天皇陵古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 この念仏寺山古墳は、西側外堤において出土した[[円筒埴輪]]片等から、[[5世紀]]前半（[[古墳時代]]中期）の築造と推定されている{{Sfn|春日率河坂上陵（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。また[[1975年]]（[[昭和]]50年）や[[2008年]]度（[[平成]]20年度）の鳥居建替の際には、江戸時代の墓地化に関連した蔵骨器・木棺が出土している{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010|pp=92-99}}[http://www.47news.jp/CN/200812/CN2008121101000797.html "開化陵周辺に江戸期の墓　宮内庁、研究者に初公開"]（共同通信、2008年12月11日記事（47NEWS））。。 なお古墳域は、後世に営まれた[[平城京]]では左京三条六坊四坪に位置する{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010|pp=92-99}}。平城京造営以前には京域内に広範囲に古墳が分布していたが、平城京造営に際して念仏寺山古墳や[[宝来山古墳]]（[[垂仁天皇]]陵）のほかは多くが破壊・削平を受けたことが知られ[https://web.archive.org/web/20130615225332/http://www.47news.jp/CN/201303/CN2013032501001891.html "平城京に壊された古墳群　大王支えた首長墓か"]（共同通信、2013年3月25日記事（47NEWS））。、本古墳はそれら消滅古墳とともに古墳群を形成したものと考えられている{{Sfn|開化天皇陵古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[明治]]期、宮内省（現・[[宮内庁]]）により開化天皇の陵に治定。 * [[1975年]]（[[昭和]]50年）、鳥居建替工事に伴う立会調査。江戸時代の蔵骨器・木棺などの出土{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010|pp=92-99}}。 * [[1976年]]（昭和51年）、外堤・渡堤工事に伴う事前調査{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010|pp=92-99}}。 * [[1988年]]（昭和63年）、透塀控柱改修工事に伴う立会調査{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010|pp=92-99}}。 * [[2002年]]（[[平成]]14年）、進入路設置その他工事に伴う立会調査{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010|pp=92-99}}。 * [[2008年]]度（平成20年度）、鳥居改築工事に伴う立会調査。蔵骨器・木棺・人骨などの出土{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010|pp=92-99}}。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|角川地名|1990|p=309}}。 * 墳丘長：約100メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：約48メートル ** 高さ：約8メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：約48メートル ** 高さ：約6メートル なお、文久の修陵に伴って墳丘規模は変化したとして、元々の墳丘長を115-120メートル程度と推測する説がある{{Sfn|開化天皇陵古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 == 被葬者 == 念仏寺山古墳の実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]では第9代'''[[開化天皇]]'''の陵に治定している[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html 天皇陵]（宮内庁）。宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/8}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）8コマ。{{Sfn|春日率川坂上陵（国史）}}。開化天皇の陵について、『[[日本書紀]]』では「春日率川坂本陵（坂上陵）」、『古事記』では「伊邪河之坂上」の所在とあるほか、『[[延喜式]]』諸陵寮では「春日率川坂上陵」として兆域は東西5段・南北5段、在京戸10烟を毎年あてる旨とともに遠陵とされ、これらが本古墳に比定されている{{Sfn|春日率川坂上陵（国史）}}。ただし[[綏靖天皇]]（第2代）から開化天皇（第9代）までの「[[欠史八代]]」と称される8代の天皇は、『日本書紀』『古事記』の諸人物の中でも極めて創作性が強い人物になる（詳細は「[[欠史八代]]」を参照）。 なお文久の修陵以前は、後円部墳頂の円丘状の高まり部分のみが開化天皇陵と見なされていたという{{Sfn|開化天皇陵古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1971|chapter=|title=奈良市史 考古編|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|pages=82-85|ref={{Harvid|奈良市史 考古編|1971}}}} * 宮内庁発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=開化天皇 春日率川坂上陵鳥居改築工事に伴う立会調査|title={{PDFlink|[https://shoryobu.kunaicho.go.jp/Publication/PDF/000/kiyo061b032.pdf 書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号]}}|publisher=[[宮内庁書陵部]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|書陵部紀要 陵墓篇 第61号|2010}}}} - リンクは宮内庁「書陵部所蔵資料目録・画像公開システム」。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref=}} *** {{Wikicite|reference=[[関晃]]「開化天皇」|ref={{Harvid|開化天皇（国史）}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=中村一郎「春日率川坂上陵」（開化天皇項目内）|ref={{Harvid|春日率川坂上陵（国史）}}}}。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=春日率河坂上陵|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|春日率河坂上陵（平凡社）|1981}}}} *** {{Wikicite|reference=刊行後版（ジャパンナレッジ収録）、2006年|ref={{Harvid|春日率河坂上陵（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}}}。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=|title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 29 奈良県|publisher=[[角川書店]]|isbn=4040012909|ref={{Harvid|角川地名|1990}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]編|author=鐘方正樹|year=2001|chapter=坂上山古墳|title=大和前方後円墳集成|publisher=[[学生社]]|isbn=4311303270|ref={{Harvid|坂上山古墳（大和前方後円墳集成）|2001}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=西崎卓哉|year=2002|chapter=開化天皇陵古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=978-4490105995|ref={{Harvid|開化天皇陵古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} * その他 ** 小倉つき子 『ドラマチック奈良』 京阪奈情報教育出版、2010年。 == 関連項目 == * [[開化天皇]] * [[率川神社]] - 開化天皇の春日率川宮（春日之伊邪河宮）伝承地。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|207421770|春日率川坂上陵}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/009/index.html 春日率川坂上陵] - 宮内庁 {{DEFAULTSORT:ねんふつしやまこふん}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:奈良市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun (念仏寺山古墳) |image = [[File:Kaika Tennnouryou.png|260px]]
Orthographic aerial view of Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun
{{small|{{Geospatial Orthographic map}}}} |alternate_name = Kōbō-yama Kofun / Sakaue-yama Kofun |Cluster = {{ill|Itsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|率川古墳群}}Dramatic Nara, p.151Refer to materials from the Nara City Board of Education, Archaeological Section. |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, Aburakōji-chō |coordinates = 34°41′0.49″N |coordinates = 135°49′26.44″E |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |map_size = Total mound length approx. 100m
(Estimated restoration: 115-120m)
Height approx. 8m (rear circular mound) |burial = Unknown |built = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Interred_individual = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, 9th emperor |burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} |Mausoleum = {{ill|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum|ja|春日率川坂上陵}} |notes = |map = Nara city |map_dot_label = Nenbutsuji-yama
Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-SSW }} [[File:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun, haisho-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}'s Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum]] '''Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun''' (念仏寺山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Aburakōji-chō|ja|油阪町}}, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is Historic Site1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "'''Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum'''" (春日率川坂上陵) and attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, the 9th emperor of Japan. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tumulus is located in central Nara City, between {{ill|Sanjo Street|ja|三条通り}} and Aburakōji Street.{{Cite book|title=Nara City Historical Records, Archaeology Volume|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1971|pages=82-85}} It has not undergone extensive excavation due to its Historic Site as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. The tumulus is oriented south-southeast. During the {{ill|Early Modern built|ja|近世}}, the tumulus fell under the temple grounds of {{ill|Nenbutsuji Temple (Nara City)|ja|念仏寺 (奈良市)}} and was utilized as a cemetery, leading to mound alterations. In the late Edo period, cemetery relocation and restoration of the mausoleum took place.{{Cite book|title=Nara Prefecture Archaeological History|publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|year=2001}} Current features include flattened tops of the circular and trapezoidal sections, and the mound's original stepped construction is no longer evident. === burial goods and Age === Fragments of cylindrical haniwa discovered on the western embankment suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century (middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}).{{Cite book|title=Historical Atlas of Japan|publisher=平凡社|year=1981}} Excavations in 1975 and 2008 uncovered Edo-period burial burial goods, including ossuary vessels and wooden coffins.[http://www.47news.jp/CN/200812/CN2008121101000797.html "burial goods from Edo built Uncovered near Emperor Kaika's Mausoleum"] (Kyodo News, December 11, 2008). During the construction of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}, many ancient Kofuns were destroyed. However, Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun and a few others, such as the {{ill|Hōraisan Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|1=Hōraiyama Kofun}}, survived.[https://web.archive.org/web/20130615225332/http://www.47news.jp/CN/201303/CN2013032501001891.html "Ancient Tombs Destroyed for Heijō-kyō built"] (Kyodo News, March 25, 2013). == Archaeological Record == * Meiji built: Designated as Emperor Kaika’s mausoleum by the Imperial Household Agency. * 1975: Excavation during torii reconstruction revealed Edo-period burial items. * 1976: Pre-construction survey of embankments. * 1988: Structural maintenance of protective fencing and gates. * 2002: Investigations during road construction. * 2008: Reconstruction of torii uncovered ossuary vessels, wooden coffins, and human remains. == Mound area == * Total length: Approx. 100 meters * Rear circular mound: ** Diameter: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 8 meters * Front trapezoidal mound: ** Width: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 6 meters == Interred_individual == The actual individual interred at Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Kaika, the 9th emperor of Japan.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Mausolea - Imperial Household Agency] Ancient records, such as the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}* and *{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}*, reference the location as "Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue," supporting this identification. However, Emperor Kaika and other rulers of the {{ill|Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|ja|欠史八代}} are considered mythological or semi-legendary figures.{{Cite book|title=Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum Overview|publisher=平凡社|year=2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dramatic Nara|author=Ogura Tsukiko|publisher=京阪奈情報教育出版|year=2010}} * {{Cite book|title=Guide to Imperial Mausolea and Ancient Tombs|author=Mori Hiroshi|publisher=筑摩書房|year=2011}} * {{Cite book|title=Continuation of Japan’s Ancient Tombs|author=Nishizaki Takuya|publisher=東京堂出版|year=2002}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}} * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|207421770|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/009/index.html Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]] [[Category:Nara Prefecture’s Kofun]] [[Category:Nara City history]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]] [[ja:念仏寺山古墳]] [[sn:enbutsuji-yama Kofun]] {{translated page|ja|念仏寺山古墳|version=99995271|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{small|{{Geospatial Orthographic map}}}} |alternate_name = Kōbō-yama Kofun / Sakaue-yama Kofun |Cluster = {{ill|Itsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|率川古墳群|qq=draft}}Dramatic Nara, p.151Refer to materials from the Nara City Board of Education, Archaeological Section. |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft}}, Aburakōji-chō |coordinates = 34°41′0.49″N |coordinates = 135°49′26.44″E |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft}} |map_size = Total mound length approx. 100m
(Estimated restoration: 115-120m)
Height approx. 8m (rear circular mound) |burial = Unknown |built = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|qq=draft}} |Interred_individual = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft}}) {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|qq=draft}}, 9th emperor |burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft}} |Mausoleum = {{ill|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum|ja|春日率川坂上陵|qq=draft}} |notes = |map = Nara city |map_dot_label = Nenbutsuji-yama
Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-SSW }} [[File:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun, haisho-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|qq=draft}}'s Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum]] '''Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun''' (念仏寺山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Aburakōji-chō|ja|油阪町|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft}}. It is Historic Site1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft}} as the "'''Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum'''" (春日率川坂上陵) and attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|qq=draft}}, the 9th emperor of Japan. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tumulus is located in central Nara City, between {{ill|Sanjo Street|ja|三条通り|qq=draft}} and Aburakōji Street.{{Cite book|title=Nara City Historical Records, Archaeology Volume|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1971|pages=82-85}} It has not undergone extensive excavation due to its Historic Site as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft}}. The tumulus is oriented south-southeast. During the {{ill|Early Modern built|ja|近世|qq=draft}}, the tumulus fell under the temple grounds of {{ill|Nenbutsuji Temple (Nara City)|ja|念仏寺 (奈良市)|qq=draft}} and was utilized as a cemetery, leading to mound alterations. In the late Edo period, cemetery relocation and restoration of the mausoleum took place.{{Cite book|title=Nara Prefecture Archaeological History|publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|year=2001}} Current features include flattened tops of the circular and trapezoidal sections, and the mound's original stepped construction is no longer evident. === burial goods and Age === Fragments of cylindrical haniwa discovered on the western embankment suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century (middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft}}).{{Cite book|title=Historical Atlas of Japan|publisher=平凡社|year=1981}} Excavations in 1975 and 2008 uncovered Edo-period burial burial goods, including ossuary vessels and wooden coffins.[http://www.47news.jp/CN/200812/CN2008121101000797.html "burial goods from Edo built Uncovered near Emperor Kaika's Mausoleum"] (Kyodo News, December 11, 2008). During the construction of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京|qq=draft}}, many ancient Kofuns were destroyed. However, Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun and a few others, such as the {{ill|Hōraisan Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|1=Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=draft}}, survived.[https://web.archive.org/web/20130615225332/http://www.47news.jp/CN/201303/CN2013032501001891.html "Ancient Tombs Destroyed for Heijō-kyō built"] (Kyodo News, March 25, 2013). == Archaeological Record == * Meiji built: Designated as Emperor Kaika’s mausoleum by the Imperial Household Agency. * 1975: Excavation during torii reconstruction revealed Edo-period burial items. * 1976: Pre-construction survey of embankments. * 1988: Structural maintenance of protective fencing and gates. * 2002: Investigations during road construction. * 2008: Reconstruction of torii uncovered ossuary vessels, wooden coffins, and human remains. == Mound area == * Total length: Approx. 100 meters * Rear circular mound: ** Diameter: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 8 meters * Front trapezoidal mound: ** Width: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 6 meters == Interred_individual == The actual individual interred at Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Kaika, the 9th emperor of Japan.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Mausolea - Imperial Household Agency] Ancient records, such as the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft}}* and *{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft}}*, reference the location as "Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue," supporting this identification. However, Emperor Kaika and other rulers of the {{ill|Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|ja|欠史八代|qq=draft}} are considered mythological or semi-legendary figures.{{Cite book|title=Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum Overview|publisher=平凡社|year=2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dramatic Nara|author=Ogura Tsukiko|publisher=京阪奈情報教育出版|year=2010}} * {{Cite book|title=Guide to Imperial Mausolea and Ancient Tombs|author=Mori Hiroshi|publisher=筑摩書房|year=2011}} * {{Cite book|title=Continuation of Japan’s Ancient Tombs|author=Nishizaki Takuya|publisher=東京堂出版|year=2002}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|207421770|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/009/index.html Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|念仏寺山古墳|version=99995271|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]] [[Category:Nara Prefecture’s Kofun]] [[Category:Nara City history]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]] [[ja:念仏寺山古墳]] [[sn:enbutsuji-yama Kofun]]

The tumulus is oriented south-southeast. During the {{ill|Early Modern built|ja|近世|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Modern built|qq=}}, the tumulus fell under the temple grounds of {{ill|Nenbutsuji Temple (Nara City)|ja|念仏寺 (奈良市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nenbutsuji Temple (Nara City)|qq=}} and was utilized as a cemetery, leading to mound alterations. In the late Edo period, cemetery relocation and restoration of the mausoleum took place.{{Cite book|title=Nara Prefecture Archaeological History|publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|year=2001}} Current features include flattened tops of the circular and trapezoidal sections, and the mound's original stepped construction is no longer evident. === burial goods and Age === Fragments of cylindrical haniwa discovered on the western embankment suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century (middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}).{{Cite book|title=Historical Atlas of Japan|publisher=平凡社|year=1981}} Excavations in 1975 and 2008 uncovered Edo-period burial burial goods, including ossuary vessels and wooden coffins.[http://www.47news.jp/CN/200812/CN2008121101000797.html "burial goods from Edo built Uncovered near Emperor Kaika's Mausoleum"] (Kyodo News, December 11, 2008). During the construction of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heijō-kyō|qq=}}, many ancient Kofuns were destroyed. However, Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun and a few others, such as the {{ill|Hōraisan Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|1=Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraisan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=}}, survived.[https://web.archive.org/web/20130615225332/http://www.47news.jp/CN/201303/CN2013032501001891.html "Ancient Tombs Destroyed for Heijō-kyō built"] (Kyodo News, March 25, 2013). == Archaeological Record == * Meiji built: Designated as Emperor Kaika’s mausoleum by the Imperial Household Agency. * 1975: Excavation during torii reconstruction revealed Edo-period burial items. * 1976: Pre-construction survey of embankments. * 1988: Structural maintenance of protective fencing and gates. * 2002: Investigations during road construction. * 2008: Reconstruction of torii uncovered ossuary vessels, wooden coffins, and human remains. == Mound area == * Total length: Approx. 100 meters * Rear circular mound: ** Diameter: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 8 meters * Front trapezoidal mound: ** Width: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 6 meters == Interred_individual == The actual individual interred at Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Kaika, the 9th emperor of Japan.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Mausolea - Imperial Household Agency] Ancient records, such as the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}* and *{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}*, reference the location as "Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue," supporting this identification. However, Emperor Kaika and other rulers of the {{ill|Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|ja|欠史八代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|qq=}} are considered mythological or semi-legendary figures.{{Cite book|title=Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum Overview|publisher=平凡社|year=2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dramatic Nara|author=Ogura Tsukiko|publisher=京阪奈情報教育出版|year=2010}} * {{Cite book|title=Guide to Imperial Mausolea and Ancient Tombs|author=Mori Hiroshi|publisher=筑摩書房|year=2011}} * {{Cite book|title=Continuation of Japan’s Ancient Tombs|author=Nishizaki Takuya|publisher=東京堂出版|year=2002}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kaika|qq=}} * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|207421770|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/009/index.html Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|念仏寺山古墳|version=99995271|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]] [[Category:Nara Prefecture’s Kofun]] [[Category:Nara City history]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]] [[ja:念仏寺山古墳]] [[sn:enbutsuji-yama Kofun]]

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun (念仏寺山古墳) |image = [[File:Kaika Tennnouryou.png|260px]]
Orthographic aerial view of Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun
{{small|{{Geospatial Orthographic map}}}} |alternate_name = Kōbō-yama Kofun / Sakaue-yama Kofun |Cluster = {{ill|Itsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|率川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}Dramatic Nara, p.151Refer to materials from the Nara City Board of Education, Archaeological Section. |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City|qq=}}, Aburakōji-chō |coordinates = 34°41′0.49″N |coordinates = 135°49′26.44″E |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} |map_size = Total mound length approx. 100m
(Estimated restoration: 115-120m)
Height approx. 8m (rear circular mound) |burial = Unknown |built = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th century|qq=}} |Interred_individual = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}}) {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kaika|qq=}}, 9th emperor |burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} |Mausoleum = {{ill|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum|ja|春日率川坂上陵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum|qq=}} |notes = |map = Nara city |map_dot_label = Nenbutsuji-yama
Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-SSW }} [[File:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun, haisho-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kaika|qq=}}'s Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum]] '''Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun''' (念仏寺山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Aburakōji-chō|ja|油阪町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aburakōji-chō|qq=}}, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City|qq=}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}. It is Historic Site1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as the "'''Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum'''" (春日率川坂上陵) and attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kaika|qq=}}, the 9th emperor of Japan. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tumulus is located in central Nara City, between {{ill|Sanjo Street|ja|三条通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanjo Street|qq=}} and Aburakōji Street.{{Cite book|title=Nara City Historical Records, Archaeology Volume|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1971|pages=82-85}} It has not undergone extensive excavation due to its Historic Site as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}}. The tumulus is oriented south-southeast. During the {{ill|Early Modern built|ja|近世|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Modern built|qq=}}, the tumulus fell under the temple grounds of {{ill|Nenbutsuji Temple (Nara City)|ja|念仏寺 (奈良市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nenbutsuji Temple (Nara City)|qq=}} and was utilized as a cemetery, leading to mound alterations. In the late Edo period, cemetery relocation and restoration of the mausoleum took place.{{Cite book|title=Nara Prefecture Archaeological History|publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|year=2001}} Current features include flattened tops of the circular and trapezoidal sections, and the mound's original stepped construction is no longer evident. === burial goods and Age === Fragments of cylindrical haniwa discovered on the western embankment suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century (middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}).{{Cite book|title=Historical Atlas of Japan|publisher=平凡社|year=1981}} Excavations in 1975 and 2008 uncovered Edo-period burial burial goods, including ossuary vessels and wooden coffins.[http://www.47news.jp/CN/200812/CN2008121101000797.html "burial goods from Edo built Uncovered near Emperor Kaika's Mausoleum"] (Kyodo News, December 11, 2008). During the construction of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heijō-kyō|qq=}}, many ancient Kofuns were destroyed. However, Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun and a few others, such as the {{ill|Hōraisan Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|1=Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraisan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=}}, survived.[https://web.archive.org/web/20130615225332/http://www.47news.jp/CN/201303/CN2013032501001891.html "Ancient Tombs Destroyed for Heijō-kyō built"] (Kyodo News, March 25, 2013). == Archaeological Record == * Meiji built: Designated as Emperor Kaika’s mausoleum by the Imperial Household Agency. * 1975: Excavation during torii reconstruction revealed Edo-period burial items. * 1976: Pre-construction survey of embankments. * 1988: Structural maintenance of protective fencing and gates. * 2002: Investigations during road construction. * 2008: Reconstruction of torii uncovered ossuary vessels, wooden coffins, and human remains. == Mound area == * Total length: Approx. 100 meters * Rear circular mound: ** Diameter: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 8 meters * Front trapezoidal mound: ** Width: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 6 meters == Interred_individual == The actual individual interred at Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Kaika, the 9th emperor of Japan.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Mausolea - Imperial Household Agency] Ancient records, such as the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}* and *{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}*, reference the location as "Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue," supporting this identification. However, Emperor Kaika and other rulers of the {{ill|Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|ja|欠史八代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|qq=}} are considered mythological or semi-legendary figures.{{Cite book|title=Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum Overview|publisher=平凡社|year=2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dramatic Nara|author=Ogura Tsukiko|publisher=京阪奈情報教育出版|year=2010}} * {{Cite book|title=Guide to Imperial Mausolea and Ancient Tombs|author=Mori Hiroshi|publisher=筑摩書房|year=2011}} * {{Cite book|title=Continuation of Japan’s Ancient Tombs|author=Nishizaki Takuya|publisher=東京堂出版|year=2002}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kaika|qq=}} * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|207421770|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/009/index.html Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|念仏寺山古墳|version=99995271|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]] [[Category:Nara City history]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]] [[ja:念仏寺山古墳]] [[sn:enbutsuji-yama Kofun]]

During the construction of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heijō-kyō|qq=}}, many ancient Kofuns were destroyed. However, Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun and a few others, such as the {{ill|Hōraisan Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|1=Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraisan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=}}, survived.[https://web.archive.org/web/20130615225332/http://www.47news.jp/CN/201303/CN2013032501001891.html "Ancient Tombs Destroyed for Heijō-kyō built"] (Kyodo News, March 25, 2013). == Archaeological Record == * Meiji built: Designated as Emperor Kaika’s mausoleum by the Imperial Household Agency. * 1975: Excavation during torii reconstruction revealed Edo-period burial items. * 1976: Pre-construction survey of embankments. * 1988: Structural maintenance of protective fencing and gates. * 2002: Investigations during road construction. * 2008: Reconstruction of torii uncovered ossuary vessels, wooden coffins, and human remains. == Mound area == * Total length: Approx. 100 meters * Rear circular mound: ** Diameter: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 8 meters * Front trapezoidal mound: ** Width: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 6 meters == Interred_individual == The actual individual interred at Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Kaika, the 9th emperor of Japan.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Mausolea - Imperial Household Agency] Ancient records, such as the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}* and *{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}*, reference the location as "Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue," supporting this identification. However, Emperor Kaika and other rulers of the {{ill|Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|ja|欠史八代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|qq=}} are considered mythological or semi-legendary figures.{{Cite book|title=Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum Overview|publisher=平凡社|year=2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dramatic Nara|author=Ogura Tsukiko|publisher=京阪奈情報教育出版|year=2010}} * {{Cite book|title=Guide to Imperial Mausolea and Ancient Tombs|author=Mori Hiroshi|publisher=筑摩書房|year=2011}} * {{Cite book|title=Continuation of Japan’s Ancient Tombs|author=Nishizaki Takuya|publisher=東京堂出版|year=2002}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kaika|qq=}} * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|207421770|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/009/index.html Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|念仏寺山古墳|version=99995271|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Nara City history]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]] [[ja:念仏寺山古墳]] [[sn:enbutsuji-yama Kofun]]

{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun (念仏寺山古墳) |image = [[File:Kaika Tennnouryou.png|260px]]
Orthographic aerial view of Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun
{{small|{{Geospatial Orthographic map}}}} |alternate_name = Kōbō-yama Kofun / Sakaue-yama Kofun |Cluster = {{ill|Itsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|率川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}Dramatic Nara, p.151Refer to materials from the Nara City Board of Education, Archaeological Section. |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City|qq=}}, Aburakōji-chō |coordinates = 34°41′0.49″N |coordinates = 135°49′26.44″E |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} |map_size = Total mound length approx. 100m
(Estimated restoration: 115-120m)
Height approx. 8m (rear circular mound) |burial = Unknown |built = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th century|qq=}} |Interred_individual = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}}) {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kaika|qq=}}, 9th emperor |burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} |Mausoleum = {{ill|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum|ja|春日率川坂上陵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum|qq=}} |notes = |map = Nara city |map_dot_label = Nenbutsuji-yama
Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-SSW }} [[File:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun, haisho-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kaika|qq=}}'s Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum]] '''Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun''' (念仏寺山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Aburakōji-chō|ja|油阪町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aburakōji-chō|qq=}}, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City|qq=}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}. It is Historic Site1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as the "'''Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum'''" (春日率川坂上陵) and attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kaika|qq=}}, the 9th emperor of Japan. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tumulus is located in central Nara City, between {{ill|Sanjo Street|ja|三条通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanjo Street|qq=}} and Aburakōji Street.{{Cite book|title=Nara City Historical Records, Archaeology Volume|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1971|pages=82-85}} It has not undergone extensive excavation due to its Historic Site as an imperial mausoleum by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}}. The tumulus is oriented south-southeast. During the {{ill|Early Modern built|ja|近世|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Modern built|qq=}}, the tumulus fell under the temple grounds of {{ill|Nenbutsuji Temple (Nara City)|ja|念仏寺 (奈良市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nenbutsuji Temple (Nara City)|qq=}} and was utilized as a cemetery, leading to mound alterations. In the late Edo period, cemetery relocation and restoration of the mausoleum took place.{{Cite book|title=Nara Prefecture Archaeological History|publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|year=2001}} Current features include flattened tops of the circular and trapezoidal sections, and the mound's original stepped construction is no longer evident. === burial goods and Age === Fragments of cylindrical haniwa discovered on the western embankment suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century (middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}).{{Cite book|title=Historical Atlas of Japan|publisher=平凡社|year=1981}} Excavations in 1975 and 2008 uncovered Edo-period burial burial goods, including ossuary vessels and wooden coffins.[http://www.47news.jp/CN/200812/CN2008121101000797.html "burial goods from Edo built Uncovered near Emperor Kaika's Mausoleum"] (Kyodo News, December 11, 2008). During the construction of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heijō-kyō|qq=}}, many ancient Kofuns were destroyed. However, Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun and a few others, such as the {{ill|Hōraisan Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|1=Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraisan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=}}, survived.[https://web.archive.org/web/20130615225332/http://www.47news.jp/CN/201303/CN2013032501001891.html "Ancient Tombs Destroyed for Heijō-kyō built"] (Kyodo News, March 25, 2013). == Archaeological Record == * Meiji built: Designated as Emperor Kaika’s mausoleum by the Imperial Household Agency. * 1975: Excavation during torii reconstruction revealed Edo-period burial items. * 1976: Pre-construction survey of embankments. * 1988: Structural maintenance of protective fencing and gates. * 2002: Investigations during road construction. * 2008: Reconstruction of torii uncovered ossuary vessels, wooden coffins, and human remains. == Mound area == * Total length: Approx. 100 meters * Rear circular mound: ** Diameter: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 8 meters * Front trapezoidal mound: ** Width: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 6 meters == Interred_individual == The actual individual interred at Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun is unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Kaika, the 9th emperor of Japan.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Mausolea - Imperial Household Agency] Ancient records, such as the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}* and *{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}*, reference the location as "Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue," supporting this identification. However, Emperor Kaika and other rulers of the {{ill|Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|ja|欠史八代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|qq=}} are considered mythological or semi-legendary figures.{{Cite book|title=Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum Overview|publisher=平凡社|year=2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dramatic Nara|author=Ogura Tsukiko|publisher=京阪奈情報教育出版|year=2010}} * {{Cite book|title=Guide to Imperial Mausolea and Ancient Tombs|author=Mori Hiroshi|publisher=筑摩書房|year=2011}} * {{Cite book|title=Continuation of Japan’s Ancient Tombs|author=Nishizaki Takuya|publisher=東京堂出版|year=2002}} == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kaika|qq=}} * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|207421770|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/009/index.html Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|念仏寺山古墳|version=99995271|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Nara City history]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]] [[ja:念仏寺山古墳]] [[sn:enbutsuji-yama Kofun]]
