{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun (念仏寺山古墳) |image = [[File:Kaika Tennnouryou.png|260px]]
Orthographic aerial view of Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun
{{small|{{Geospatial Orthographic map}}}} |alternate_name = Kōbō-yama Kofun / Sakaue-yama Kofun |Cluster = {{ill|Itsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|率川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}Dramatic Nara, p.151Refer to materials from the Nara City Board of Education, Archaeological Section. |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], Aburakōji-chō |coordinates = 34°41′0.49″N |coordinates = 135°49′26.44″E |omitted = JP-29 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |map_size = Total mound length approx. 100m
(Estimated restoration: 115-120m)
Height approx. 8m (rear circular mound) |burial = Unknown |built = Early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |Interred_individual = (Designated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], 9th emperor |burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} |Mausoleum = {{ill|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum|ja|春日率川坂上陵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum|qq=}} |notes = |map = Nara city |map_dot_label = Nenbutsuji-yama
Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-SSW }} [[File:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun, haisho-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]'s Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum]] '''Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun''' (念仏寺山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Aburakōji-chō|ja|油阪町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aburakōji-chō|qq=}}, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "'''Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum'''" (春日率川坂上陵) and attributed to [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], the 9th emperor of Japan. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tumulus is located in central Nara City, between {{ill|Sanjo Street|ja|三条通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanjo Street|qq=}} and Aburakōji Street.{{Cite book|title=Nara City Historical Records, Archaeology Volume|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1971|pages=82-85}} It has not undergone extensive excavation due to its Historic Site as an imperial mausoleum by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]. The tumulus is oriented south-southeast. During the [[:en:Early modern period|Early Modern built]], the tumulus fell under the temple grounds of {{ill|Nenbutsuji Temple (Nara City)|ja|念仏寺 (奈良市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nenbutsuji Temple (Nara City)|qq=}} and was utilized as a cemetery, leading to mound alterations. In the late Edo period, cemetery relocation and restoration of the mausoleum took place.{{Cite book|title=Nara Prefecture Archaeological History|publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|year=2001}} Current features include flattened tops of the circular and trapezoidal sections, and the mound's original stepped construction is no longer evident. === burial goods and Age === Fragments of cylindrical haniwa discovered on the western embankment suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century (middle [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]).{{Cite book|title=Historical Atlas of Japan|publisher=平凡社|year=1981}} Excavations in 1975 and 2008 uncovered Edo-period burial burial goods, including ossuary vessels and wooden coffins.[http://www.47news.jp/CN/200812/CN2008121101000797.html "burial goods from Edo built Uncovered near Emperor Kaika's Mausoleum"] (Kyodo News, December 11, 2008). During the construction of [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]], many ancient Kofuns were destroyed. However, Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun and a few others, such as the {{ill|Hōraisan Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|1=Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraisan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=}}, survived.[https://web.archive.org/web/20130615225332/http://www.47news.jp/CN/201303/CN2013032501001891.html "Ancient Tombs Destroyed for Heijō-kyō built"] (Kyodo News, March 25, 2013). == Archaeological Record == * Meiji built: Designated as Emperor Kaika’s mausoleum by the Imperial Household Agency. * 1975: Excavation during torii reconstruction revealed Edo-period burial items. * 1976: Pre-construction survey of embankments. * 1988: Structural maintenance of protective fencing and gates. * 2002: Investigations during road construction. * 2008: Reconstruction of torii uncovered ossuary vessels, wooden coffins, and human remains. == Mound area == * Total length: Approx. 100 meters * Rear circular mound: ** Diameter: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 8 meters * Front trapezoidal mound: ** Width: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 6 meters == Interred_individual == The actual individual interred at Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun is unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Kaika, the 9th emperor of Japan.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Mausolea - Imperial Household Agency] Ancient records, such as the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]* and *[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]*, reference the location as "Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue," supporting this identification. However, Emperor Kaika and other rulers of the {{ill|Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|ja|欠史八代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|qq=}} are considered mythological or semi-legendary figures.{{Cite book|title=Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum Overview|publisher=平凡社|year=2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dramatic Nara|author=Ogura Tsukiko|publisher=京阪奈情報教育出版|year=2010}} * {{Cite book|title=Guide to Imperial Mausolea and Ancient Tombs|author=Mori Hiroshi|publisher=筑摩書房|year=2011}} * {{Cite book|title=Continuation of Japan’s Ancient Tombs|author=Nishizaki Takuya|publisher=東京堂出版|year=2002}} == See Also == * [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|207421770|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/009/index.html Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|念仏寺山古墳|version=99995271|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Nara City history]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]] [[ja:念仏寺山古墳]] [[sn:enbutsuji-yama Kofun]] [[Category:奈良市の歴史]]

However, Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun and a few others, such as the {{ill|Hōraisan Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|1=Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraisan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=}}, survived.[https://web.archive.org/web/20130615225332/http://www.47news.jp/CN/201303/CN2013032501001891.html "Ancient Tombs Destroyed for Heijō-kyō built"] (Kyodo News, March 25, 2013). == Archaeological Record == * Meiji built: Designated as Emperor Kaika’s mausoleum by the Imperial Household Agency. * 1975: Excavation during torii reconstruction revealed Edo-period burial items. * 1976: Pre-construction survey of embankments. * 1988: Structural maintenance of protective fencing and gates. * 2002: Investigations during road construction. * 2008: Reconstruction of torii uncovered ossuary vessels, wooden coffins, and human remains. == Mound area == * Total length: Approx. 100 meters * Rear circular mound: ** Diameter: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 8 meters * Front trapezoidal mound: ** Width: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 6 meters == Interred_individual == The actual individual interred at Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun is unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Kaika, the 9th emperor of Japan.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Mausolea - Imperial Household Agency] Ancient records, such as the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]* and *[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]*, reference the location as "Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue," supporting this identification. However, Emperor Kaika and other rulers of the {{ill|Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|ja|欠史八代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|qq=}} are considered mythological or semi-legendary figures.{{Cite book|title=Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum Overview|publisher=平凡社|year=2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dramatic Nara|author=Ogura Tsukiko|publisher=京阪奈情報教育出版|year=2010}} * {{Cite book|title=Guide to Imperial Mausolea and Ancient Tombs|author=Mori Hiroshi|publisher=筑摩書房|year=2011}} * {{Cite book|title=Continuation of Japan’s Ancient Tombs|author=Nishizaki Takuya|publisher=東京堂出版|year=2002}} == See Also == * [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|207421770|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/009/index.html Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|念仏寺山古墳|version=99995271|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Nara City history]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]] [[ja:念仏寺山古墳]] [[sn:enbutsuji-yama Kofun]] [[Category:奈良市の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]]

revision.comment: Bot: replace redirect category → Kofun of Nara prefecture
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun (念仏寺山古墳) |image = [[File:Kaika Tennnouryou.png|260px]]
Orthographic aerial view of Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun
{{small|{{Geospatial Orthographic map}}}} |alternate_name = Kōbō-yama Kofun / Sakaue-yama Kofun |Cluster = {{ill|Itsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|率川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}Dramatic Nara, p.151Refer to materials from the Nara City Board of Education, Archaeological Section. |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], Aburakōji-chō |coordinates = 34°41′0.49″N |coordinates = 135°49′26.44″E |omitted = JP-29 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |map_size = Total mound length approx. 100m
(Estimated restoration: 115-120m)
Height approx. 8m (rear circular mound) |burial = Unknown |built = Early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |Interred_individual = (Designated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], 9th emperor |burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} |Mausoleum = {{ill|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum|ja|春日率川坂上陵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum|qq=}} |notes = |map = Nara city |map_dot_label = Nenbutsuji-yama
Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-SSW }} [[File:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun, haisho-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]'s Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum]] '''Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun''' (念仏寺山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Aburakōji-chō|ja|油阪町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aburakōji-chō|qq=}}, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "'''Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum'''" (春日率川坂上陵) and attributed to [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], the 9th emperor of Japan. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tumulus is located in central Nara City, between {{ill|Sanjo Street|ja|三条通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanjo Street|qq=}} and Aburakōji Street.{{Cite book|title=Nara City Historical Records, Archaeology Volume|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1971|pages=82-85}} It has not undergone extensive excavation due to its Historic Site as an imperial mausoleum by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]. The tumulus is oriented south-southeast. During the [[:en:Early modern period|Early Modern built]], the tumulus fell under the temple grounds of {{ill|Nenbutsuji Temple (Nara City)|ja|念仏寺 (奈良市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nenbutsuji Temple (Nara City)|qq=}} and was utilized as a cemetery, leading to mound alterations. In the late Edo period, cemetery relocation and restoration of the mausoleum took place.{{Cite book|title=Nara Prefecture Archaeological History|publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|year=2001}} Current features include flattened tops of the circular and trapezoidal sections, and the mound's original stepped construction is no longer evident. === burial goods and Age === Fragments of cylindrical haniwa discovered on the western embankment suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century (middle [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]).{{Cite book|title=Historical Atlas of Japan|publisher=平凡社|year=1981}} Excavations in 1975 and 2008 uncovered Edo-period burial burial goods, including ossuary vessels and wooden coffins.[http://www.47news.jp/CN/200812/CN2008121101000797.html "burial goods from Edo built Uncovered near Emperor Kaika's Mausoleum"] (Kyodo News, December 11, 2008). During the construction of [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]], many ancient Kofuns were destroyed. However, Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun and a few others, such as the {{ill|Hōraisan Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|1=Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraisan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=}}, survived.[https://web.archive.org/web/20130615225332/http://www.47news.jp/CN/201303/CN2013032501001891.html "Ancient Tombs Destroyed for Heijō-kyō built"] (Kyodo News, March 25, 2013). == Archaeological Record == * Meiji built: Designated as Emperor Kaika’s mausoleum by the Imperial Household Agency. * 1975: Excavation during torii reconstruction revealed Edo-period burial items. * 1976: Pre-construction survey of embankments. * 1988: Structural maintenance of protective fencing and gates. * 2002: Investigations during road construction. * 2008: Reconstruction of torii uncovered ossuary vessels, wooden coffins, and human remains. == Mound area == * Total length: Approx. 100 meters * Rear circular mound: ** Diameter: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 8 meters * Front trapezoidal mound: ** Width: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 6 meters == Interred_individual == The actual individual interred at Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun is unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Kaika, the 9th emperor of Japan.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Mausolea - Imperial Household Agency] Ancient records, such as the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]* and *[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]*, reference the location as "Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue," supporting this identification. However, Emperor Kaika and other rulers of the {{ill|Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|ja|欠史八代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|qq=}} are considered mythological or semi-legendary figures.{{Cite book|title=Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum Overview|publisher=平凡社|year=2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dramatic Nara|author=Ogura Tsukiko|publisher=京阪奈情報教育出版|year=2010}} * {{Cite book|title=Guide to Imperial Mausolea and Ancient Tombs|author=Mori Hiroshi|publisher=筑摩書房|year=2011}} * {{Cite book|title=Continuation of Japan’s Ancient Tombs|author=Nishizaki Takuya|publisher=東京堂出版|year=2002}} == See Also == * [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|207421770|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/009/index.html Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|念仏寺山古墳|version=99995271|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Nara City history]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]] [[ja:念仏寺山古墳]] [[sn:enbutsuji-yama Kofun]] [[Category:奈良市の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]]

Height approx. 8m (rear circular mound) |burial = Unknown |built = Early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |Interred_individual = (Designated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], 9th emperor |burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} |Mausoleum = {{ill|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum|ja|春日率川坂上陵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum|qq=}} |notes = |map = Nara city |map_dot_label = Nenbutsuji-yama
Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-SSW }} [[File:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun, haisho-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]'s Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum]] '''Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun''' (念仏寺山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Aburakōji-chō|ja|油阪町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aburakōji-chō|qq=}}, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "'''Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum'''" (春日率川坂上陵) and attributed to [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], the 9th emperor of Japan. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tumulus is located in central Nara City, between {{ill|Sanjo Street|ja|三条通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanjo Street|qq=}} and Aburakōji Street.{{Cite book|title=Nara City Historical Records, Archaeology Volume|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1971|pages=82-85}} It has not undergone extensive excavation due to its Historic Site as an imperial mausoleum by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]. The tumulus is oriented south-southeast. During the [[:en:Early modern period|Early Modern built]], the tumulus fell under the temple grounds of {{ill|Nenbutsuji Temple (Nara City)|ja|念仏寺 (奈良市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nenbutsuji Temple (Nara City)|qq=}} and was utilized as a cemetery, leading to mound alterations. In the late Edo period, cemetery relocation and restoration of the mausoleum took place.{{Cite book|title=Nara Prefecture Archaeological History|publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|year=2001}} Current features include flattened tops of the circular and trapezoidal sections, and the mound's original stepped construction is no longer evident. === burial goods and Age === Fragments of cylindrical haniwa discovered on the western embankment suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century (middle [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]).{{Cite book|title=Historical Atlas of Japan|publisher=平凡社|year=1981}} Excavations in 1975 and 2008 uncovered Edo-period burial burial goods, including ossuary vessels and wooden coffins.[http://www.47news.jp/CN/200812/CN2008121101000797.html "burial goods from Edo built Uncovered near Emperor Kaika's Mausoleum"] (Kyodo News, December 11, 2008). During the construction of [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]], many ancient Kofuns were destroyed. However, Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun and a few others, such as the {{ill|Hōraisan Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|1=Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraisan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=}}, survived.[https://web.archive.org/web/20130615225332/http://www.47news.jp/CN/201303/CN2013032501001891.html "Ancient Tombs Destroyed for Heijō-kyō built"] (Kyodo News, March 25, 2013). == Archaeological Record == * Meiji built: Designated as Emperor Kaika’s mausoleum by the Imperial Household Agency. * 1975: Excavation during torii reconstruction revealed Edo-period burial items. * 1976: Pre-construction survey of embankments. * 1988: Structural maintenance of protective fencing and gates. * 2002: Investigations during road construction. * 2008: Reconstruction of torii uncovered ossuary vessels, wooden coffins, and human remains. == Mound area == * Total length: Approx. 100 meters * Rear circular mound: ** Diameter: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 8 meters * Front trapezoidal mound: ** Width: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 6 meters == Interred_individual == The actual individual interred at Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun is unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Kaika, the 9th emperor of Japan.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Mausolea - Imperial Household Agency] Ancient records, such as the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]* and *[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]*, reference the location as "Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue," supporting this identification. However, Emperor Kaika and other rulers of the {{ill|Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|ja|欠史八代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|qq=}} are considered mythological or semi-legendary figures.{{Cite book|title=Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum Overview|publisher=平凡社|year=2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dramatic Nara|author=Ogura Tsukiko|publisher=京阪奈情報教育出版|year=2010}} * {{Cite book|title=Guide to Imperial Mausolea and Ancient Tombs|author=Mori Hiroshi|publisher=筑摩書房|year=2011}} * {{Cite book|title=Continuation of Japan’s Ancient Tombs|author=Nishizaki Takuya|publisher=東京堂出版|year=2002}} == See Also == * [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|207421770|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/009/index.html Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|念仏寺山古墳|version=99995271|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Nara City history]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]] [[ja:念仏寺山古墳]] [[sn:enbutsuji-yama Kofun]] [[Category:History of Nara�]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] {{Commons category|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} [[zh:念佛寺山古墳]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Fixed Kofun templates
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun (念仏寺山古墳) |image = [[File:Kaika Tennnouryou.png|260px]]
Orthographic aerial view of Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun
{{small|{{Geospatial Orthographic map}}}} |alternate_name = Kōbō-yama Kofun / Sakaue-yama Kofun |Cluster = {{ill|Itsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|率川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}Dramatic Nara, p.151Refer to materials from the Nara City Board of Education, Archaeological Section. |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], Aburakōji-chō |coordinates = 34°41′0.49″N |coordinates = 135°49′26.44″E |omitted = JP-29 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |map_size = Total mound length approx. 100m
(Estimated restoration: 115-120m)
Height approx. 8m (rear circular mound) |burial = Unknown |built = Early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |Interred_individual = (Designated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], 9th emperor |burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} |Mausoleum = {{ill|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum|ja|春日率川坂上陵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 開化天皇}} |notes = |map = Nara city |map_dot_label = Nenbutsuji-yama
Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-SSW }} [[File:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun, haisho-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]'s Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum]] '''Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun''' (念仏寺山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Aburakōji-chō|ja|油阪町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aburakōji-chō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "'''Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum'''" (春日率川坂上陵) and attributed to [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], the 9th emperor of Japan. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tumulus is located in central Nara City, between {{ill|Sanjo Street|ja|三条通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanjo Street|qq=}} and Aburakōji Street.{{Cite book|title=Nara City Historical Records, Archaeology Volume|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1971|pages=82-85}} It has not undergone extensive excavation due to its Historic Site as an imperial mausoleum by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]. The tumulus is oriented south-southeast. During the [[:en:Early modern period|Early Modern built]], the tumulus fell under the temple grounds of {{ill|Nenbutsuji Temple (Nara City)|ja|念仏寺 (奈良市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nenbutsuji Temple (Nara City)|qq=}} and was utilized as a cemetery, leading to mound alterations. In the late Edo period, cemetery relocation and restoration of the mausoleum took place.{{Cite book|title=Nara Prefecture Archaeological History|publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|year=2001}} Current features include flattened tops of the circular and trapezoidal sections, and the mound's original stepped construction is no longer evident. === burial goods and Age === Fragments of cylindrical haniwa discovered on the western embankment suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century (middle [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]).{{Cite book|title=Historical Atlas of Japan|publisher=平凡社|year=1981}} Excavations in 1975 and 2008 uncovered Edo-period burial burial goods, including ossuary vessels and wooden coffins.[http://www.47news.jp/CN/200812/CN2008121101000797.html "burial goods from Edo built Uncovered near Emperor Kaika's Mausoleum"] (Kyodo News, December 11, 2008). During the construction of [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]], many ancient Kofuns were destroyed. However, Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun and a few others, such as the {{ill|Hōraisan Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|1=Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraisan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=}}, survived.[https://web.archive.org/web/20130615225332/http://www.47news.jp/CN/201303/CN2013032501001891.html "Ancient Tombs Destroyed for Heijō-kyō built"] (Kyodo News, March 25, 2013). == Archaeological Record == * Meiji built: Designated as Emperor Kaika’s mausoleum by the Imperial Household Agency. * 1975: Excavation during torii reconstruction revealed Edo-period burial items. * 1976: Pre-construction survey of embankments. * 1988: Structural maintenance of protective fencing and gates. * 2002: Investigations during road construction. * 2008: Reconstruction of torii uncovered ossuary vessels, wooden coffins, and human remains. == Mound area == * Total length: Approx. 100 meters * Rear circular mound: ** Diameter: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 8 meters * Front trapezoidal mound: ** Width: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 6 meters == Interred_individual == The actual individual interred at Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun is unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Kaika, the 9th emperor of Japan.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Mausolea - Imperial Household Agency] Ancient records, such as the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]* and *[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]*, reference the location as "Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue," supporting this identification. However, Emperor Kaika and other rulers of the {{ill|Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|ja|欠史八代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|qq=}} are considered mythological or semi-legendary figures.{{Cite book|title=Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum Overview|publisher=平凡社|year=2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dramatic Nara|author=Ogura Tsukiko|publisher=京阪奈情報教育出版|year=2010}} * {{Cite book|title=Guide to Imperial Mausolea and Ancient Tombs|author=Mori Hiroshi|publisher=筑摩書房|year=2011}} * {{Cite book|title=Continuation of Japan’s Ancient Tombs|author=Nishizaki Takuya|publisher=東京堂出版|year=2002}} == See Also == * [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|207421770|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/009/index.html Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|念仏寺山古墳|version=99995271|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Nara City history]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]] [[ja:念仏寺山古墳]] [[sn:enbutsuji-yama Kofun]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] {{Commons category|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} [[zh:念佛寺山古墳]]

During the construction of [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]], many ancient Kofuns were destroyed. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Kaika, the 9th emperor of Japan.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Mausolea - Imperial Household Agency] Ancient records, such as the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]* and *[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]*, reference the location as "Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue," supporting this identification. However, Emperor Kaika and other rulers of the {{ill|Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|ja|欠史八代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|qq=}} are considered mythological or semi-legendary figures.{{Cite book|title=Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum Overview|publisher=平凡社|year=2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dramatic Nara|author=Ogura Tsukiko|publisher=京阪奈情報教育出版|year=2010}} * {{Cite book|title=Guide to Imperial Mausolea and Ancient Tombs|author=Mori Hiroshi|publisher=筑摩書房|year=2011}} * {{Cite book|title=Continuation of Japan’s Ancient Tombs|author=Nishizaki Takuya|publisher=東京堂出版|year=2002}} == See Also == * [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|207421770|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/009/index.html Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|念仏寺山古墳|version=99995271|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Nara City history]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]] [[ja:念仏寺山古墳]] [[sn:enbutsuji-yama Kofun]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] {{Commons category|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} [[zh:念佛寺山古墳]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun (念仏寺山古墳) |image = [[File:Kaika Tennnouryou.png|260px]]
Orthographic aerial view of Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun
{{small|{{Geospatial Orthographic map}}}} |alternate_name = Kōbō-yama Kofun / Sakaue-yama Kofun |Cluster = {{ill|Itsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|率川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}Dramatic Nara, p.151Refer to materials from the Nara City Board of Education, Archaeological Section. |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], Aburakōji-chō |coordinates = 34°41′0.49″N |coordinates = 135°49′26.44″E |omitted = JP-29 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |map_size = Total mound length approx. 100m
(Estimated restoration: 115-120m)
Height approx. 8m (rear circular mound) |burial = Unknown |built = Early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |Interred_individual = (Designated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], 9th emperor |burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} |Mausoleum = {{ill|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum|ja|春日率川坂上陵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 開化天皇}} |notes = |map = Nara city |map_dot_label = Nenbutsuji-yama
Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-SSW }} [[File:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun, haisho-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]'s Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum]] '''Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun''' (念仏寺山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Aburakōji-chō|ja|油阪町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aburakōji-chō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "'''Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum'''" (春日率川坂上陵) and attributed to [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], the 9th emperor of Japan. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tumulus is located in central Nara City, between {{ill|Sanjo Street|ja|三条通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanjo Street|qq=}} and Aburakōji Street.{{Cite book|title=Nara City Historical Records, Archaeology Volume|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1971|pages=82-85}} It has not undergone extensive excavation due to its Historic Site as an imperial mausoleum by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]. The tumulus is oriented south-southeast. During the [[:en:Early modern period|Early Modern built]], the tumulus fell under the temple grounds of {{ill|Nenbutsuji Temple (Nara City)|ja|念仏寺 (奈良市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nenbutsuji Temple (Nara City)|qq=}} and was utilized as a cemetery, leading to mound alterations. In the late Edo period, cemetery relocation and restoration of the mausoleum took place.{{Cite book|title=Nara Prefecture Archaeological History|publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|year=2001}} Current features include flattened tops of the circular and trapezoidal sections, and the mound's original stepped construction is no longer evident. === burial goods and Age === Fragments of cylindrical haniwa discovered on the western embankment suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century (middle [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]).{{Cite book|title=Historical Atlas of Japan|publisher=平凡社|year=1981}} Excavations in 1975 and 2008 uncovered Edo-period burial burial goods, including ossuary vessels and wooden coffins.[http://www.47news.jp/CN/200812/CN2008121101000797.html "burial goods from Edo built Uncovered near Emperor Kaika's Mausoleum"] (Kyodo News, December 11, 2008). During the construction of [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]], many ancient Kofuns were destroyed. However, Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun and a few others, such as the {{ill|Hōraisan Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|1=Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraisan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=}}, survived.[https://web.archive.org/web/20130615225332/http://www.47news.jp/CN/201303/CN2013032501001891.html "Ancient Tombs Destroyed for Heijō-kyō built"] (Kyodo News, March 25, 2013). == Archaeological Record == * Meiji built: Designated as Emperor Kaika’s mausoleum by the Imperial Household Agency. * 1975: Excavation during torii reconstruction revealed Edo-period burial items. * 1976: Pre-construction survey of embankments. * 1988: Structural maintenance of protective fencing and gates. * 2002: Investigations during road construction. * 2008: Reconstruction of torii uncovered ossuary vessels, wooden coffins, and human remains. == Mound area == * Total length: Approx. 100 meters * Rear circular mound: ** Diameter: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 8 meters * Front trapezoidal mound: ** Width: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 6 meters == Interred_individual == The actual individual interred at Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun is unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Kaika, the 9th emperor of Japan.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Mausolea - Imperial Household Agency] Ancient records, such as the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]* and *[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]*, reference the location as "Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue," supporting this identification. However, Emperor Kaika and other rulers of the {{ill|Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|ja|欠史八代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|qq=}} are considered mythological or semi-legendary figures.{{Cite book|title=Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum Overview|publisher=平凡社|year=2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dramatic Nara|author=Ogura Tsukiko|publisher=京阪奈情報教育出版|year=2010}} * {{Cite book|title=Guide to Imperial Mausolea and Ancient Tombs|author=Mori Hiroshi|publisher=筑摩書房|year=2011}} * {{Cite book|title=Continuation of Japan’s Ancient Tombs|author=Nishizaki Takuya|publisher=東京堂出版|year=2002}} == See Also == * [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|207421770|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/009/index.html Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|念仏寺山古墳|version=99995271|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Nara City history]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]] [[ja:念仏寺山古墳]] [[sn:enbutsuji-yama Kofun]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] {{Commons category|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} [[zh:念佛寺山古墳]]

Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-SSW }} [[File:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun, haisho-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]'s Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum]] '''Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun''' (念仏寺山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Aburakōji-chō|ja|油阪町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aburakōji-chō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "'''Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum'''" (春日率川坂上陵) and attributed to [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], the 9th emperor of Japan. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tumulus is located in central Nara City, between {{ill|Sanjo Street|ja|三条通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanjo Street|qq=}} and Aburakōji Street.{{Cite book|title=Nara City Historical Records, Archaeology Volume|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1971|pages=82-85}} It has not undergone extensive excavation due to its Historic Site as an imperial mausoleum by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]. The tumulus is oriented south-southeast. During the [[:en:Early modern period|Early Modern built]], the tumulus fell under the temple grounds of {{ill|Nenbutsuji Temple (Nara City)|ja|念仏寺 (奈良市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nenbutsuji Temple (Nara City)|qq=}} and was utilized as a cemetery, leading to mound alterations. In the late Edo period, cemetery relocation and restoration of the mausoleum took place.{{Cite book|title=Nara Prefecture Archaeological History|publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|year=2001}} Current features include flattened tops of the circular and trapezoidal sections, and the mound's original stepped construction is no longer evident. === burial goods and Age === Fragments of cylindrical haniwa discovered on the western embankment suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century (middle [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]).{{Cite book|title=Historical Atlas of Japan|publisher=平凡社|year=1981}} Excavations in 1975 and 2008 uncovered Edo-period burial burial goods, including ossuary vessels and wooden coffins.[http://www.47news.jp/CN/200812/CN2008121101000797.html "burial goods from Edo built Uncovered near Emperor Kaika's Mausoleum"] (Kyodo News, December 11, 2008). During the construction of [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]], many ancient Kofuns were destroyed. However, Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun and a few others, such as the {{ill|Hōraisan Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|1=Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraisan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=}}, survived.[https://web.archive.org/web/20130615225332/http://www.47news.jp/CN/201303/CN2013032501001891.html "Ancient Tombs Destroyed for Heijō-kyō built"] (Kyodo News, March 25, 2013). == Archaeological Record == * Meiji built: Designated as Emperor Kaika’s mausoleum by the Imperial Household Agency. * 1975: Excavation during torii reconstruction revealed Edo-period burial items. * 1976: Pre-construction survey of embankments. * 1988: Structural maintenance of protective fencing and gates. * 2002: Investigations during road construction. * 2008: Reconstruction of torii uncovered ossuary vessels, wooden coffins, and human remains. == Mound area == * Total length: Approx. 100 meters * Rear circular mound: ** Diameter: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 8 meters * Front trapezoidal mound: ** Width: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 6 meters == Interred_individual == The actual individual interred at Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun is unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Kaika, the 9th emperor of Japan.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Mausolea - Imperial Household Agency] Ancient records, such as the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]* and *[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]*, reference the location as "Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue," supporting this identification. However, Emperor Kaika and other rulers of the {{ill|Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|ja|欠史八代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|qq=}} are considered mythological or semi-legendary figures.{{Cite book|title=Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum Overview|publisher=平凡社|year=2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dramatic Nara|author=Ogura Tsukiko|publisher=京阪奈情報教育出版|year=2010}} * {{Cite book|title=Guide to Imperial Mausolea and Ancient Tombs|author=Mori Hiroshi|publisher=筑摩書房|year=2011}} * {{Cite book|title=Continuation of Japan’s Ancient Tombs|author=Nishizaki Takuya|publisher=東京堂出版|year=2002}} == See Also == * [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|207421770|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/009/index.html Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|念仏寺山古墳|version=99995271|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]] [[ja:念仏寺山古墳]] [[sn:enbutsuji-yama Kofun]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] {{Commons category|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} [[zh:念佛寺山古墳]]

However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Kaika, the 9th emperor of Japan.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Mausolea - Imperial Household Agency] Ancient records, such as the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]* and *[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]*, reference the location as "Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue," supporting this identification. However, Emperor Kaika and other rulers of the {{ill|Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|ja|欠史八代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|qq=}} are considered mythological or semi-legendary figures.{{Cite book|title=Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum Overview|publisher=平凡社|year=2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dramatic Nara|author=Ogura Tsukiko|publisher=京阪奈情報教育出版|year=2010}} * {{Cite book|title=Guide to Imperial Mausolea and Ancient Tombs|author=Mori Hiroshi|publisher=筑摩書房|year=2011}} * {{Cite book|title=Continuation of Japan’s Ancient Tombs|author=Nishizaki Takuya|publisher=東京堂出版|year=2002}} == See Also == * [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|207421770|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/009/index.html Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|念仏寺山古墳|version=99995271|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]] [[ja:念仏寺山古墳]] [[sn:enbutsuji-yama Kofun]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] {{Commons category|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} [[zh:念佛寺山古墳]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q22119913]] Wikidata name: Nenbutsujiyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun (念仏寺山古墳) |image = File:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun, haisho-1.jpg |alternate_name = Kōbō-yama Kofun / Sakaue-yama Kofun |Cluster = {{ill|Itsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|率川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}Dramatic Nara, p.151Refer to materials from the Nara City Board of Education, Archaeological Section. |location = {{ill|Nara|qid=Q169134}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.683469|N|135.824011|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |omitted = JP-29 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |map_size = Total mound length approx. 100m
(Estimated restoration: 115-120m)
Height approx. 8m (rear circular mound) |burial = Unknown |built = Early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |Interred_individual = (Designated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], 9th emperor |burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} |Mausoleum = {{ill|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum|ja|春日率川坂上陵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 開化天皇|1=Emperor Kaika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q333548}} |notes = |map = Nara city |map_dot_label = Nenbutsuji-yama
Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-SSW |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718}} |commemorates = {{ill|Emperor Kaika|qid=Q333548}} }} [[File:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun, haisho-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]'s Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum]] '''Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun''' (念仏寺山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Aburakōji-chō|ja|油阪町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aburakōji-chō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "'''Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum'''" (春日率川坂上陵) and attributed to [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], the 9th emperor of Japan. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tumulus is located in central Nara City, between {{ill|Sanjo Street|ja|三条通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanjo Street|qq=|1=Sanjō Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355862}} and Aburakōji Street.{{Cite book|title=Nara City Historical Records, Archaeology Volume|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1971|pages=82-85}} It has not undergone extensive excavation due to its Historic Site as an imperial mausoleum by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]. The tumulus is oriented south-southeast. During the [[:en:Early modern period|Early Modern built]], the tumulus fell under the temple grounds of {{ill|Nenbutsuji Temple (Nara City)|ja|念仏寺 (奈良市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nenbutsuji Temple (Nara City)|qq=}} and was utilized as a cemetery, leading to mound alterations. In the late Edo period, cemetery relocation and restoration of the mausoleum took place.{{Cite book|title=Nara Prefecture Archaeological History|publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|year=2001}} Current features include flattened tops of the circular and trapezoidal sections, and the mound's original stepped construction is no longer evident. === burial goods and Age === Fragments of cylindrical haniwa discovered on the western embankment suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century (middle [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]).{{Cite book|title=Historical Atlas of Japan|publisher=平凡社|year=1981}} Excavations in 1975 and 2008 uncovered Edo-period burial burial goods, including ossuary vessels and wooden coffins.[http://www.47news.jp/CN/200812/CN2008121101000797.html "burial goods from Edo built Uncovered near Emperor Kaika's Mausoleum"] (Kyodo News, December 11, 2008). During the construction of [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]], many ancient Kofuns were destroyed. However, Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun and a few others, such as the {{ill|Hōraisan Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|1=Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraisan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=}}, survived.[https://web.archive.org/web/20130615225332/http://www.47news.jp/CN/201303/CN2013032501001891.html "Ancient Tombs Destroyed for Heijō-kyō built"] (Kyodo News, March 25, 2013). == Archaeological Record == * Meiji built: Designated as Emperor Kaika’s mausoleum by the Imperial Household Agency. * 1975: Excavation during torii reconstruction revealed Edo-period burial items. * 1976: Pre-construction survey of embankments. * 1988: Structural maintenance of protective fencing and gates. * 2002: Investigations during road construction. * 2008: Reconstruction of torii uncovered ossuary vessels, wooden coffins, and human remains. == Mound area == * Total length: Approx. 100 meters * Rear circular mound: ** Diameter: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 8 meters * Front trapezoidal mound: ** Width: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 6 meters == Interred_individual == The actual individual interred at Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun is unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Kaika, the 9th emperor of Japan.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Mausolea - Imperial Household Agency] Ancient records, such as the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]* and *[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]*, reference the location as "Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue," supporting this identification. However, Emperor Kaika and other rulers of the {{ill|Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|ja|欠史八代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|qq=|1=Kesshi Hachidai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q838196}} are considered mythological or semi-legendary figures.{{Cite book|title=Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum Overview|publisher=平凡社|year=2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dramatic Nara|author=Ogura Tsukiko|publisher=京阪奈情報教育出版|year=2010}} * {{Cite book|title=Guide to Imperial Mausolea and Ancient Tombs|author=Mori Hiroshi|publisher=筑摩書房|year=2011}} * {{Cite book|title=Continuation of Japan’s Ancient Tombs|author=Nishizaki Takuya|publisher=東京堂出版|year=2002}} == See Also == * [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|207421770|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/009/index.html Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|念仏寺山古墳|version=99995271|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun]] [[ja:念仏寺山古墳]] [[sn:enbutsuji-yama Kofun]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] {{Commons category|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} [[zh:念佛寺山古墳]]

Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-SSW |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} |commemorates = {{ill|Emperor Kaika|qid=Q333548}} }} [[File:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun, haisho-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]'s Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum]] '''Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun''' (念仏寺山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Aburakōji-chō|ja|油阪町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aburakōji-chō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "'''Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum'''" (春日率川坂上陵) and attributed to [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], the 9th emperor of Japan. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tumulus is located in central Nara City, between {{ill|Sanjo Street|ja|三条通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanjo Street|qq=|1=Sanjō Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355862}} and Aburakōji Street.{{Cite book|title=Nara City Historical Records, Archaeology Volume|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1971|pages=82-85}} It has not undergone extensive excavation due to its Historic Site as an imperial mausoleum by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]. The tumulus is oriented south-southeast. During the [[:en:Early modern period|Early Modern built]], the tumulus fell under the temple grounds of {{ill|Nenbutsuji Temple (Nara City)|ja|念仏寺 (奈良市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nenbutsuji Temple (Nara City)|qq=}} and was utilized as a cemetery, leading to mound alterations. In the late Edo period, cemetery relocation and restoration of the mausoleum took place.{{Cite book|title=Nara Prefecture Archaeological History|publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|year=2001}} Current features include flattened tops of the circular and trapezoidal sections, and the mound's original stepped construction is no longer evident. === burial goods and Age === Fragments of cylindrical haniwa discovered on the western embankment suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century (middle [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]).{{Cite book|title=Historical Atlas of Japan|publisher=平凡社|year=1981}} Excavations in 1975 and 2008 uncovered Edo-period burial burial goods, including ossuary vessels and wooden coffins.[http://www.47news.jp/CN/200812/CN2008121101000797.html "burial goods from Edo built Uncovered near Emperor Kaika's Mausoleum"] (Kyodo News, December 11, 2008). During the construction of [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]], many ancient Kofuns were destroyed. However, Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun and a few others, such as the {{ill|Hōraisan Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|1=Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraisan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=}}, survived.[https://web.archive.org/web/20130615225332/http://www.47news.jp/CN/201303/CN2013032501001891.html "Ancient Tombs Destroyed for Heijō-kyō built"] (Kyodo News, March 25, 2013). == Archaeological Record == * Meiji built: Designated as Emperor Kaika’s mausoleum by the Imperial Household Agency. * 1975: Excavation during torii reconstruction revealed Edo-period burial items. * 1976: Pre-construction survey of embankments. * 1988: Structural maintenance of protective fencing and gates. * 2002: Investigations during road construction. * 2008: Reconstruction of torii uncovered ossuary vessels, wooden coffins, and human remains. == Mound area == * Total length: Approx. 100 meters * Rear circular mound: ** Diameter: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 8 meters * Front trapezoidal mound: ** Width: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 6 meters == Interred_individual == The actual individual interred at Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun is unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Kaika, the 9th emperor of Japan.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Mausolea - Imperial Household Agency] Ancient records, such as the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]* and *[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]*, reference the location as "Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue," supporting this identification. However, Emperor Kaika and other rulers of the {{ill|Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|ja|欠史八代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|qq=|1=Kesshi Hachidai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q838196}} are considered mythological or semi-legendary figures.{{Cite book|title=Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum Overview|publisher=平凡社|year=2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dramatic Nara|author=Ogura Tsukiko|publisher=京阪奈情報教育出版|year=2010}} * {{Cite book|title=Guide to Imperial Mausolea and Ancient Tombs|author=Mori Hiroshi|publisher=筑摩書房|year=2011}} * {{Cite book|title=Continuation of Japan’s Ancient Tombs|author=Nishizaki Takuya|publisher=東京堂出版|year=2002}} == See Also == * [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|207421770|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/009/index.html Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|念仏寺山古墳|version=99995271|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:念仏寺山古墳]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] {{Commons category|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} [[zh:念佛寺山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q22119913}}

However, Emperor Kaika and other rulers of the {{ill|Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|ja|欠史八代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|qq=|1=Kesshi Hachidai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q838196}} are considered mythological or semi-legendary figures.{{Cite book|title=Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum Overview|publisher=平凡社|year=2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dramatic Nara|author=Ogura Tsukiko|publisher=京阪奈情報教育出版|year=2010}} * {{Cite book|title=Guide to Imperial Mausolea and Ancient Tombs|author=Mori Hiroshi|publisher=筑摩書房|year=2011}} * {{Cite book|title=Continuation of Japan’s Ancient Tombs|author=Nishizaki Takuya|publisher=東京堂出版|year=2002}} == See Also == * [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|207421770|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/009/index.html Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|念仏寺山古墳|version=99995271|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:念仏寺山古墳]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] {{Commons category|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} [[zh:念佛寺山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q22119913}}

This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun (念仏寺山古墳) |image = File:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun, haisho-1.jpg |alternate_name = Kōbō-yama Kofun / Sakaue-yama Kofun |Cluster = {{ill|Itsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|率川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}Dramatic Nara, p.151Refer to materials from the Nara City Board of Education, Archaeological Section. |location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.683469|N|135.824011|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |omitted = JP-29 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |map_size = Total mound length approx. 100m

Height approx. 8m (rear circular mound) |burial = Unknown |built = Early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |Interred_individual = (Designated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], 9th emperor |burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} |Mausoleum = {{ill|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum|ja|春日率川坂上陵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 開化天皇|1=Emperor Kaika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q333548}} |notes = |map = Nara city |map_dot_label = Nenbutsuji-yama
Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-SSW |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} |commemorates = {{ill|Emperor Kaika|WD=Q333548}} }} [[File:Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun, haisho-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]'s Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum]] '''Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun''' (念仏寺山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Aburakōji-chō|ja|油阪町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aburakōji-chō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "'''Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum'''" (春日率川坂上陵) and attributed to [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], the 9th emperor of Japan. == Overview == This large keyhole-shaped tumulus is located in central Nara City, between {{ill|Sanjo Street|ja|三条通り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanjo Street|qq=|1=Sanjō Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355862}} and Aburakōji Street.{{Cite book|title=Nara City Historical Records, Archaeology Volume|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1971|pages=82-85}} It has not undergone extensive excavation due to its Historic Site as an imperial mausoleum by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]. The tumulus is oriented south-southeast. During the [[:en:Early modern period|Early Modern built]], the tumulus fell under the temple grounds of {{ill|Nenbutsuji Temple (Nara City)|ja|念仏寺 (奈良市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nenbutsuji Temple (Nara City)|qq=}} and was utilized as a cemetery, leading to mound alterations. In the late Edo period, cemetery relocation and restoration of the mausoleum took place.{{Cite book|title=Nara Prefecture Archaeological History|publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|year=2001}} Current features include flattened tops of the circular and trapezoidal sections, and the mound's original stepped construction is no longer evident. === burial goods and Age === Fragments of cylindrical haniwa discovered on the western embankment suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century (middle [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]).{{Cite book|title=Historical Atlas of Japan|publisher=平凡社|year=1981}} Excavations in 1975 and 2008 uncovered Edo-period burial burial goods, including ossuary vessels and wooden coffins.[http://www.47news.jp/CN/200812/CN2008121101000797.html "burial goods from Edo built Uncovered near Emperor Kaika's Mausoleum"] (Kyodo News, December 11, 2008). During the construction of [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō-kyō]], many ancient Kofuns were destroyed. However, Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun and a few others, such as the {{ill|Hōraisan Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|1=Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraisan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=}}, survived.[https://web.archive.org/web/20130615225332/http://www.47news.jp/CN/201303/CN2013032501001891.html "Ancient Tombs Destroyed for Heijō-kyō built"] (Kyodo News, March 25, 2013). == Archaeological Record == * Meiji built: Designated as Emperor Kaika’s mausoleum by the Imperial Household Agency. * 1975: Excavation during torii reconstruction revealed Edo-period burial items. * 1976: Pre-construction survey of embankments. * 1988: Structural maintenance of protective fencing and gates. * 2002: Investigations during road construction. * 2008: Reconstruction of torii uncovered ossuary vessels, wooden coffins, and human remains. == Mound area == * Total length: Approx. 100 meters * Rear circular mound: ** Diameter: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 8 meters * Front trapezoidal mound: ** Width: Approx. 48 meters ** Height: Approx. 6 meters == Interred_individual == The actual individual interred at Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun is unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the mausoleum of Emperor Kaika, the 9th emperor of Japan.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html List of Imperial Mausolea - Imperial Household Agency] Ancient records, such as the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]* and *[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]*, reference the location as "Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue," supporting this identification. However, Emperor Kaika and other rulers of the {{ill|Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|ja|欠史八代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Undocumented Sovereigns|qq=|1=Kesshi Hachidai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q838196}} are considered mythological or semi-legendary figures.{{Cite book|title=Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum Overview|publisher=平凡社|year=2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dramatic Nara|author=Ogura Tsukiko|publisher=京阪奈情報教育出版|year=2010}} * {{Cite book|title=Guide to Imperial Mausolea and Ancient Tombs|author=Mori Hiroshi|publisher=筑摩書房|year=2011}} * {{Cite book|title=Continuation of Japan’s Ancient Tombs|author=Nishizaki Takuya|publisher=東京堂出版|year=2002}} == See Also == * [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] * {{ill|Isagawa Shrine|ja|率川神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isagawa Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} {{osm box|w|207421770|Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/009/index.html Kasuga Itsukawa Sakaue Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|念仏寺山古墳|version=99995271|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:念仏寺山古墳]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] {{Commons category|Nenbutsuji-yama Kofun}} [[zh:念佛寺山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q22119913}}
